dataset: whole-cell systems (bacteria to human, conceptual)

problem: Human whole-cell dynamical models are a central goal of systems biology but face significant challenges in feasibility and implementation.
method: Analysis of existing pathway models, community survey, and reflection on bacterial whole-cell modeling experience to propose the Human Whole-Cell Modeling Project.
finding: Human whole-cell models are rapidly becoming feasible; a plan based on technology development, standards, and interdisciplinary collaboration is proposed.
dataset: human cells (conceptual)

problem: How can physical, molecular-level whole-cell models be constructed and simulated to connect molecular structure with biological function?
method: Review of quantitative whole-cell model construction approaches, from kinetic network models to physical molecular-level simulations.
finding: Building physical whole-cell models at molecular detail is becoming possible, though significant challenges remain in construction and simulation.
dataset: cellular molecular composition and structure data

problem: Continuum modeling approaches lack the detail and realism needed to track single-cell behaviors and emergent macroscopic effects in bacterial populations.
method: Agent-based modeling framework (BSim 2.0) with capsular cell geometry, physical/chemical interactions, realistic growth model, DDE solver, and realistic environmental geometries.
finding: BSim 2.0 enables detailed simulations of bacterial dynamics in microfluidic environments with physically interacting cells and realistic growth.
dataset: bacterial populations in microfluidic chemostats

problem: Development of bioprocess digital twins for mammalian cell culture in advanced biomanufacturing.
dataset: mammalian cell culture bioprocesses

problem: Rule-based models of multivalent/multistate molecules need compartmental and spatial simulation capabilities within integrated modeling frameworks.
method: Extension of Virtual Cell (VCell) with compartmental and spatial rule-based modeling, integrating BioNetGen, NFSim, PDE solvers, and Smoldyn stochastic simulator.
finding: VCell now supports seamless compartmental and spatial rule-based simulations, connecting reaction networks from rules to explicit geometries for reaction-diffusion equations.
dataset: molecular interaction networks (multivalent/multistate molecules)

problem: How do stochastic cell size control and division noise affect intracellular gene expression dynamics beyond the chemical master equation paradigm?
method: Analytical agent-based framework of growing and dividing cells with exact simulation algorithm; extended linear noise approximation for agent-based models.
finding: The chemical master equation agrees with agent-based formulation under stochastic concentration homeostasis; when violated, gene expression noise can qualitatively deviate from CME predictions.
dataset: gene expression networks in growing cell populations (theoretical)
metric: noise levels (coefficient of variation), moment analysis
baseline: chemical master equation with static extrinsic noise
result: exact agreement under stochastic concentration homeostasis condition

problem: In vitro concentration-viability data need to be interpreted in terms of actual cellular exposure concentrations rather than nominal concentrations.
method: Process-based Virtual Cell Based Assay (VCBA) integrating fate/transport, cell partitioning, growth/division, toxicity, and experimental setup models.
finding: The VCBA can simulate medium and intracellular concentrations for designing in vitro experiments and performing in vitro to in vivo extrapolation, supporting chemical risk assessment without animal testing.
dataset: cell-based in vitro assay systems
metric: concentration-viability curves

problem: Bus voltage disturbance and power flow management in fuel cell-based DC microgrids clusters require coordinated control with virtual inertia.
method: Multi-agent system (MAS) control framework with virtual inertia for primary/secondary/tertiary control layers; real-time simulation platform.
finding: The proposed method achieves bus voltage smooth control, SoC consistency, load abrupt response, and plug-and-play capability in fuel cell DC microgrid clusters.
dataset: fuel cell-based DC microgrids cluster (simulation)
metric: bus voltage stability, battery SoC convergence
result: SoC convergence in finite time

problem: Predicting cell response to substrate topography lacks robust computational frameworks, requiring many experimental iterations.
method: Multicomponent virtual cell computational model predicting whole cell and nucleus characteristics (shape, direction, chromatin conformation) on various substrates.
finding: The virtual cell model can predict mesenchymal stem cell behavior on different substrates, providing a high-throughput approach for optimized substrate development.
dataset: mesenchymal stem cells on engineered substrates
metric: cell shape, direction, chromatin conformation correlation with experiments
result: qualitative agreement with MSC experimental outcomes

problem: How to estimate parameters and states for heterogeneous cell population models from experimental data?
method: Review of estimation methods for population balance equation models, stochastic models, and individual-based models, including observability and identifiability analysis.
finding: A comprehensive overview of estimation methods is provided, highlighting the need to match data characteristics with appropriate modeling and estimation frameworks.
dataset: heterogeneous cell populations (various experimental data types)
metric: observability, identifiability

problem: Non-destructive and accurate monitoring of mold contamination in foodstuffs is needed to prevent economic losses and mycotoxin production.
method: Whole-cell biosensor array (14 E. coli stress-responsive promoters) coupled with machine-learning classifiers (6 algorithms compared); GC-MS volatile marker identification.
finding: Random forest classifiers achieved 100% accuracy discriminating healthy from moldy samples, and 95-98% accuracy for pre-mold stage discrimination.
dataset: peanuts and maize (mold-contaminated foodstuffs)
metric: classification accuracy
baseline: 6 ML algorithms compared (RF selected as optimal)
result: 100% healthy vs moldy; 95-98% pre-mold staging; 83% cross-food discrimination

problem: Detection of Fe3+ ions in live cells using supramolecular network polymers as bioimaging agents.
dataset: HeLa cells

problem: Integrating experiments, theory, and simulations into whole-cell models.
Understanding the effects of repeated whole cigarette smoke exposure on bronchial epithelial differentiation and inflammatory response.
method: Air-liquid interface 3D co-culture model (epithelial cells + fibroblasts in collagen matrix) with repeated whole cigarette smoke exposure; histological and gene expression analyses.
finding: Repeated CS exposure decreased ciliated cells, perturbed goblet cell differentiation, and augmented secretion of IL-8, GRO-α, IL-1β, and GM-CSF with increasing exposure repetition.
dataset: human bronchial epithelial tissue model (in vitro 3D co-culture)
metric: cell differentiation markers, cytokine secretion levels
result: significant increase in IL-8, GRO-α, IL-1β, GM-CSF secretion

problem: Developing effective T1 MRI contrast agents for in vitro and in vivo stem cell labeling and tracking.
method: Ultrasmall graphene oxide PEGylated and conjugated with DOTA-Gd complexes (GO-DOTA-Gd); MRI relaxivity measurements and hMSC labeling experiments.
finding: GO-DOTA-Gd achieved ~3x higher T1 relaxivity than commercial Magnevist, effectively labeled hMSCs without adverse effects, and enabled intracranial detection of labeled cells in vivo.
dataset: human mesenchymal stem cells (hMSCs)
metric: T1 relaxivity (r1), cell viability, proliferation, differentiation
result: r1=14.2 mM⁻¹s⁻¹ at 11.7T (~3x Magnevist); in vivo detection of 5×10⁵ hMSCs

problem: Determining cytotoxic and genotoxic effects of glyphosate and its formulations at human-relevant exposure concentrations.
method: Whole blood cytotoxicity assays and cancer cell line (HEC1A, MCF7, MDA-MB-231) cytotoxicity/genotoxicity assays with pure glyphosate, Roundup, and Wipeout at 10-500 µg/ml.
finding: Differential toxicity profiles observed: glyphosate formulations showed varied cytotoxicity depending on cell estrogen receptor status; DNA damage detected in HEC1A and MDA-MB-231 at 500 µg/ml.
dataset: human whole blood; HEC1A, MCF7, MDA-MB-231 cancer cell lines
metric: cell viability, DNA damage (genotoxicity)
result: non-monotonic toxicity at 10 µg/ml; DNA damage at 500 µg/ml in HEC1A and MDA-MB-231

problem: Developing skin-friendly nanoemulsion carriers incorporating traditional herbal wound healing agents for improved wound care.
method: Lecithin-based O/W nanoemulsions with betulin extract (BET) and purified spruce balm (PSB); MTT, BrdU, and scratch assays on primary human skin cells.
finding: BET and PSB-loaded nanoemulsions showed higher cell viability, proliferation, and significantly better wound closure rates than controls including dexpanthenol-loaded NEs.
dataset: primary human fibroblasts and keratinocytes
metric: cell viability (MTT), proliferation (BrdU), wound closure rate (scratch assay)
result: BET and PSB NEs significantly outperformed dexpanthenol positive control in wound closure

problem: Understanding the mechanism of RPE pathology in Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome (SLOS), an autosomal recessive disease caused by defective cholesterol synthesis.
method: SLOS rat model (AY9944-treated) and iPSC-derived SLOS RPE cell culture; western blot, autophagy markers, phagosome-lysosome fusion analysis.
finding: Defective phagosome maturation (failure to fuse with lysosomes) underlies RPE pathology in SLOS, with accumulation of immature autophagosomes and elevated bisretinoids.
dataset: SLOS rat retina; human iPSC-derived SLOS RPE cells
metric: A2E/bisretinoid levels, MAP1LC3B-II/SQSTM1 levels, lysosomal pH, CTSD activity
baseline: untreated rats; iPSC-derived normal human RPE
result: increased A2E, altered autophagy markers, failed phagosome-lysosome fusion

problem: Rule-based modeling requires learning complex scripting languages, hindering broader adoption for large reaction networks.
method: Graphical rule-based model specification integrated into Virtual Cell (VCell) with BioNetGen and NFSim simulation engines; ODE, stochastic, and network-free solvers.
finding: VCell provides seamless graphical rule-based modeling with multiple simulation engines, eliminating the need to learn scripting languages for rule-based models.
dataset: molecular interaction networks (general)

problem: Simulation of cells and microscope images is needed for development, validation, and selection of image analysis algorithms in cytometry.
method: Review of simulation approaches from parametric cell models to learning-based synthesis and multi-stage simulation for complex scenes with image formation laws.
finding: Virtual cell imaging spans particle to tissue scales and supports subcellular detection to cell tracking; validation approaches and future directions are discussed.
dataset: simulated cell images (various scales)

problem: Evaluating and benchmarking virtual cell models that predict cellular responses to perturbations lacks standardized frameworks.
method: Recurring open benchmark competition (Virtual Cell Challenge) from Arc Institute with purpose-built evaluation framework and datasets.
finding: The Virtual Cell Challenge aims to catalyze progress toward a 'Turing test' for virtual cells by providing standardized benchmarks for cell state prediction models.
dataset: purpose-built perturbation response datasets
metric: perturbation response prediction accuracy

problem: Modeling cell and tumor growth using off-lattice agent-based approaches.
dataset: cell and tumor systems

problem: Forecasting multistep-ahead culture profiles of mammalian cell culture for bioprocess digital twins requires identifying optimal data-driven model configurations.
method: Systematic comparison of multistep forecasting strategies, input features, and AI algorithms for predicting viable cell density, mAb titer, glucose, lactate, and ammonia.
finding: Practical guidelines provided for choosing best DDM combinations balancing computational load with accuracy; applicable to interactive digital twin implementation.
dataset: CHO cell culture mAb production (hypothetical in-line datasets)
metric: forecasting accuracy (viable cell density, mAb titer, metabolite concentrations)

problem: Creating the most detailed computational model of an E. coli cell to better understand and predict cellular behavior.
method: Whole-cell modeling framework with multi-algorithmic approach integrating genomic, metabolic, and regulatory sub-models.
finding: The E. coli whole-cell modeling project describes its approach, framework, and current model version for comprehensive cellular prediction.
dataset: Escherichia coli

problem: Microscopic pedestrian evacuation models are computationally expensive while macroscopic approaches lack flexibility for individual decision-making.
method: Agent-based pedestrian cell transmission model (A-PCTM) combining microscopic agent decisions with macroscopic CTM computational efficiency; adaptive direction-based path finding.
finding: A-PCTM shows flexibility in destination switching and travel direction choice depending on situational conditions, combining advantages of both approaches.
dataset: pedestrian evacuation simulations (various geometric settings)
metric: computational efficiency, routing flexibility

problem: Understanding how Myxococcus xanthus cells aggregate into multicellular mounds in response to starvation.
method: Agent-based model with point-particle cells; tested chemotaxis with adaptation vs traffic-jam mechanisms for biased movement toward aggregates.
finding: A chemotaxis model with adaptation reproduces experimentally observed stable aggregate formation and cell alignment patterns around aggregates.
dataset: Myxococcus xanthus
metric: pattern formation, cell alignment around aggregates
result: reproduction of observed aggregation dynamics and alignment patterns

problem: Predicting evolution of multicellular systems requires mathematical modeling, but current approaches are inaccessible to non-specialists.
method: Cell behavior hypothesis grammar using natural language statements (cell rules) to create mathematical models; integration with multi-omics data for in silico virtual experiments.
finding: The grammar enables systematic creation of multicellular models, demonstrated in cancer and brain development, democratizing virtual cell laboratories for the broader community.
dataset: cancer tissues, brain development (multi-omics)

problem: Integrating cryo-electron tomography structural data with stochastic whole-cell simulations is challenging but offers insights into how cell structure affects biochemical dynamics.
method: GPU-based spatially resolved stochastic simulations using cryo-ET derived cell geometries; demonstrated with genetic switch in S. cerevisiae and gene repression in HeLa cell.
finding: Cryo-ET geometry can be converted to simulation environments for whole-cell stochastic models, revealing how cellular substructure affects stochastic reaction dynamics.
dataset: Saccharomyces cerevisiae, HeLa cell (cryo-ET tomograms)

problem: Estimating half-cell thermodynamic parameters from whole-cell Li-ion battery experiments with low error.
method: Multi-species multi-reaction (MSMR) half-cell model adapted for whole-cell cycling; sequential least-square optimization; bootstrap analysis over 600 cycles.
finding: Low-error fits achieved for OCV and differential voltage curves; identifies specific insertion reactions responsible for capacity loss and whole-cell slippage.
dataset: Samsung 18650 NMC|C Li-ion cells (fresh and aged, 600 cycles)
metric: mean absolute error (MAE) of cell voltage
result: under 5 mV MAE for charge/discharge OCV curves

problem: Estimating effective diffusivity of blowing agents in closed-cell low-density polyurethane foams.
dataset: closed-cell low-density polyurethane foams

problem: A complete description of cell state requires integrating size, shape, components, reactions, and environment over time, which is overwhelming for dynamical whole-cell models.
method: Review of current challenges in obtaining sufficient information for each aspect of dynamical whole-cell models (DWCM) of bacterial systems.
finding: Progress is being made on many fronts toward integrating cellular and molecular structures and dynamics into whole-cell models for well-studied bacterial systems.
dataset: bacterial cells (well-studied systems)

problem: Visualizing and cross-comparing single-cell RNAseq dimension reduction representations is challenging, especially in 3D.
method: CellexalVR: fully interactive virtual reality environment for single-cell experiment visualization and analysis.
finding: CellexalVR enables intuitive and collaborative exploration of single-cell data in an immersive VR environment.
dataset: single-cell RNAseq datasets

problem: The benefits of cellular heterogeneity and stochasticity in cGAS-mediated IFNβ innate immune signaling are unclear.
method: Agent-based model simulating DNA transfection and viral infection with varying heterogeneity and IFNβ stochasticity levels.
finding: Heterogeneity enhances IFNβ responses during infection; only 20-30% of cells need to respond for optimal protection, balancing immune potency with minimized cell death.
dataset: cGAS-IFNβ pathway (simulated viral infection)
metric: cell death rate, viral load reduction
result: 20-30% responding cells optimal; beyond provides no additional protection

problem: Understanding interactions between a polymer electrolyte membrane fuel cell and boost converter using multiscale modeling.
dataset: PEM fuel cell with boost converter

problem: Existing whole-cell models represent only small bacteria; new scalable methods are needed for human cells with 100x more species and 10,000-100,000x more molecules.
method: Novel system design with expressive sequence- and rule-based modeling language and multi-algorithm simulator using optimistic parallel discrete event simulation.
finding: The proposed scalable architecture enables building whole-cell models of human cells by addressing computational challenges of multi-algorithm simulation at scale.
dataset: human cells (conceptual, vs bacterial WC model)
metric: scalability (molecular species count, molecule count)
result: 100x species, 10,000-100,000x molecules scalability target

problem: Optimal formation of virtual manufacturing cells considering material handling costs, machine installation, production costs, and multi-skilled workforce assignment.
method: Bi-objective mathematical model for dynamic virtual cell formation, solved with fuzzy goal programming and genetic algorithm for large-scale instances.
finding: The proposed GA approach effectively solves large-scale dynamic virtual cell formation problems considering multiple cost objectives and workforce skills.
dataset: manufacturing cell formation (numerical examples)
metric: material handling cost, production cost, machine installation cost

problem: Monte Carlo simulations of BKCa-CaV ion channel complexes are computationally expensive and unsuitable for large-scale whole-cell electrical activity simulations.
method: Markov chain analysis of BKCa-CaV complexes with 1:n stoichiometry; timescale-based derivation of concise whole-cell BKCa current model.
finding: BKCa-CaV stoichiometry can substantially affect whole-cell electrical activity; the concise model respects local interactions while enabling efficient whole-cell simulations.
dataset: BKCa-CaV ion channel complexes in various cell types
metric: whole-cell BKCa current, electrical activity patterns
result: computationally efficient model matching stochastic simulation results

problem: Cancer immunotherapy is limited by immunosuppressive factors in the tumour microenvironment, including cancer-associated fibroblasts expressing FAPα.
method: Xenogeneic whole cell tumour vaccine (WCTV) expressing human FAPα transduced into murine tumour cells; tested in multiple solid tumour models.
finding: Xenogeneic FAPα-based WCTV effectively delayed tumour growth, prevented recurrence, and drove both T cell and antibody responses against cancer cells and CAFs.
dataset: murine solid tumour models (multiple)
metric: tumour growth delay, recurrence prevention, antigen-specific CTL response
result: effective tumour growth delay and recurrence prevention; FAPα-specific autoantibodies detected

problem: Simulating long-term repeated dose toxic effects of chemicals in vitro to support risk assessment and reduce animal testing.
method: Extended Virtual Cell Based Assay (VCBA) for repeated exposure, implemented in KNIME workflow; tested with caffeine and amiodarone in HepG2 and HepaRG liver cell lines.
finding: The VCBA can adequately predict repeated exposure experiments in liver cell lines, supporting design of long-term in vitro experiments and practical risk assessment applications.
dataset: HepG2 and HepaRG liver cell lines; caffeine and amiodarone
metric: cell viability under repeated exposure
result: adequate prediction of repeated dose effects

problem: The pathological mechanisms underlying metal fume fever, cardiovascular effects, and lung damage from inhaled metal oxide nanoparticles are not fully understood.
method: In vitro exposure of human alveolar epithelial cells, vascular endothelial cells, and whole blood to characterized gunshot smoke metal particles (0.1–50 μg/ml), measuring ROS, cytokines, and coagulation markers.
finding: Metal particles induced ROS and pro-inflammatory cytokines in lung cells, activated endothelial cells via non-ROS mechanisms, and triggered platelet activation and blood coagulation in whole blood, orchestrating thrombogenesis.
dataset: Human alveolar epithelial cells, vascular endothelial cells, whole blood; gunshot smoke particles
metric: ROS production, cytokine levels (IL-8, GM-CSF, IL-6), sICAM-1, sCD40L, PDGF-BB, VEGF-A

problem: Simulating in vitro chemical fate following acute exposure using computational approaches.
method: Virtual Cell Based Assay (VCBA) simulation model for chemical fate prediction.
dataset: In vitro chemical exposure data

problem: Understanding carcinoma reversion mechanisms through computational modeling.
method: Virtual cell modeling of carcinoma reversion.
dataset: Carcinoma cells

problem: Developing novel Schiff base ligand-metal complexes as potential cholesterol lowering and anticancer agents.
method: Synthesis, characterization, antimicrobial testing, ADME profiling, and computational studies of Schiff base transition metal complexes.
dataset: HeLa cells

problem: Simulating the biophysics of glioblastoma multiforme cell migration and oxygen field evolution.
method: Agent-based hybrid model combining cell biophysics with oxygen field dynamics.
finding: In collective migration, cluster survival prevails over cell mitosis regardless of group size, delaying necrotic foci formation and reducing oxygen consumption rate.
dataset: Glioblastoma multiforme cells (simulated)

problem: Understanding the biomechanical behavior of malaria-infected red blood cells across multiple scales.
method: Multiscale computational modeling of malaria-infected red blood cells.
dataset: Malaria-infected red blood cells (Plasmodium falciparum)

problem: Modeling and simulating an integrated energy system combining copper-chlorine cycle, molten carbonate fuel cell, and heat recovery.
method: MATLAB/SIMULINK simulation with PID controller for copper-chlorine cycle, molten carbonate fuel cell, regenerative steam cycle, and electric heater.
dataset: Copper-chlorine thermochemical cycle, molten carbonate fuel cell system

problem: Developing a generalized whole-cell model for wastewater-fed microbial fuel cells.
method: Whole-cell mathematical modeling of microbial fuel cell performance.
dataset: Wastewater-fed microbial fuel cells

problem: Evaluating the best in vitro liver models including whole-cell systems and transporter function for toxicity testing.
method: Expert discussion and review of in vitro liver model systems.
dataset: In vitro liver models

problem: Designing minimal genomes is limited by lack of appropriate design tools and biological knowledge, requiring expensive experimental iterations.
method: Two minimal genome design algorithms (GAMA and Minesweeper) using whole-cell model simulations of Mycoplasma genitalium on supercomputers.
finding: Produced thousands of in silico minimal genomes by combining minimization algorithms with whole-cell model simulations, demonstrating feasibility of computational genome design.
dataset: Mycoplasma genitalium whole-cell model (28 cellular submodels)
result: 1000s of in silico minimal genomes generated

problem: Forming virtual cells in manufacturing using clustering approaches.
method: Fuzzy c-means clustering for virtual cell formation.
dataset: Manufacturing cell formation data

problem: Frequent handoffs and limited wireless backhaul capacity in ultradense 5G networks cause high packet loss rates and signaling overhead.
method: Machine learning approach using particle swarm optimization (PSO) with modified K-means algorithm and weighted distance parameter for flexible virtual cell design.
finding: Optimal virtual cell solutions found efficiently through considerably fewer iterations compared to exhaustive search, balancing packet loss rate and handoff overhead.
dataset: Ultradense network (5G) simulation scenarios
metric: Packet loss rate, handoff overhead, number of iterations
result: Considerably fewer iterations than exhaustive search

problem: Building a virtual cell requires comprehensive understanding of protein network dynamics, necessitating large-scale perturbation proteome data and intelligent computational models.
method: Neural ordinary differential equation-based foundation model (ProteinTalks) pretrained on >38 million perturbed protein measurements in breast cancer cell lines.
finding: ProteinTalks robustly predicts drug efficacy and synergy, identifies novel drug combinations, uncovers resistance-associated proteins, and enhances prognosis prediction when integrated with clinical data.
dataset: 38M+ perturbed protein measurements in breast cancer cell lines, patient-derived tumor xenografts, clinical patient data
metric: Predictive accuracy across downstream tasks
result: Consistently improved predictive accuracy across various downstream tasks

problem: Simulating the motion and interactions of cells in whole blood requires geometric reconstruction of elastic surfaces in immersed boundary methods.
method: Immersed boundary method with parameter-free radial basis functions for high-order meshless parametric reconstruction of point clouds and elastic force computations.
finding: Red blood cells are crucial for understanding platelet motion; vessel wall geometry has negligible effect in presence of RBCs. A novel platelet 'unicycling' motion was discovered in 3D simulations.
dataset: Simulated whole blood (RBCs, platelets, endothelium)

problem: Understanding how initial concentration of low molecular weight molecules affects cellular noise during transport across the E. coli cell envelope.
method: Novel agent-based model simulating single-molecule transport across the cell envelope of E. coli.
finding: Cellular noise during molecule diffusion is concentration-dependent and size-independent.
dataset: E. coli cell envelope
metric: Cellular noise
result: Noise is concentration-dependent, size-independent

problem: Simulating mammalian cell culture bioreactor processes using high-performance computational approaches.
method: Agent-based modeling of mammalian cell culture bioreactor.
dataset: Mammalian cell culture bioreactor

problem: Understanding the utility of mathematical and computational tools in addressing questions of biological importance in NK cell biology and the immune system.
method: Survey and evaluation of mathematical and computational modeling approaches applied to NK cell biology.
finding: Quantitative tools are increasingly valuable for providing biological insights in NK cell biology, though ambivalence in the biology community persists.
dataset: Natural Killer (NK) cells, immune system

problem: Understanding how Plasmodium falciparum infection and maturation affect the large deformation behavior of red blood cells.
method: 3D multiscale meshfree (element-free) method simulating optical tweezers experiments on malaria-infected red blood cells.
finding: Deformability of malaria-infected cells decreases significantly as infection progresses; simulation results agree well with experimental data.
dataset: Malaria-infected red blood cells (Plasmodium falciparum)
metric: Force-displacement response
result: Good agreement with experimental data; significant deformability decrease with infection progression

problem: Understanding how light-induced changes affect excitation energy transfer in photosystems of cyanobacteria, particularly high-light tolerance mechanisms.
method: Spectroscopic assessment of whole cells from Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 and Synechococcus elongatus UTEX 2973 grown under high and low light conditions.
finding: Synechococcus 2973 employs an uncommon photoprotection strategy possibly involving zeaxanthin via an OCP homolog, downregulates phycobilisome antenna under high light, and has more tightly regulated PSII/PSI stoichiometric changes.
dataset: Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803, Synechococcus elongatus UTEX 2973
metric: Excitation energy transfer, PSII/PSI stoichiometry
baseline: Low light growth condition; Synechocystis 6803 as comparison

problem: Integrating various cellular subsystems and predicting comprehensive cellular behaviors on a genome-wide scale remains a major challenge for whole-cell modeling.
method: Review of whole-cell modeling approaches including model construction, simulation, and analysis techniques for prokaryotes, yeasts, and eukaryotic cells.
finding: Whole-cell modeling has been applied to diverse organisms and applications including drug discovery and controlled differentiation, with extensions to multiscale and multicellular modeling.
dataset: Prokaryotes, yeasts, higher eukaryotic cells

problem: Exploring whether deterministic models can reproduce single-channel and whole-cell ionic currents traditionally modeled with stochastic Markov approaches.
method: Modified deterministic model based on Liebovitch and Toth applied to a voltage-dependent K+ channel, compared with two-state Markov model.
finding: The modified deterministic model reproduces both single-channel currents simulated by a two-state Markov model and whole-cell currents from a population of K+ channels.
dataset: Voltage-dependent K+ channel (simulated)
baseline: Two-state Markov stochastic model (Liebovitch and Toth 1991)
result: Successfully reproduces both single-channel and whole-cell currents

problem: The mechanisms of force-dependent regulation of cell-substrate adhesions are unclear due to spatially heterogeneous distribution of endogenous forces within cells.
method: Micropatterning technique on square patterns with immunostaining and immunoblot analysis of paxillin phosphorylation in U2OS cells, combined with pharmacological force inhibition and a minimal mathematical model.
finding: Paxillin phosphorylation responds oppositely to mechanical forces at subcellular vs whole-cell levels; a minimal model reconciles this by showing negative local regulation combined with force-dependent protein assembly.
dataset: Human osteosarcoma U2OS cells on square micropatterns
metric: Paxillin phosphorylation level (immunostaining, immunoblot)
result: Opposite mechano-response between subcellular and whole-cell levels

problem: Analyzing slow motion of couple stress fluid past a solid sphere in a virtual cell with slip effects.
method: Analytical/numerical modeling of couple stress fluid dynamics with slip boundary conditions in a virtual cell geometry.
dataset: Couple stress fluid, solid sphere in virtual cell

problem: Predicting chemical toxicity in 3D cell cultures for risk assessment, extending existing 2D models.
method: Extension of the 2D Virtual Cell Based Assay (VCBA) to a 3D spheroid model using quasi-diffusion process with concentric cell layers.
finding: The 3D VCBA model simulates close correlation between compound concentration influence and HepaRG 3D cell culture viability, showing promising agreement with in vitro data.
dataset: HepaRG 3D spheroid cell culture; acetaminophen (APAP)
metric: Cell viability correlation with compound concentration
baseline: 2D VCBA model; empirical in vitro 3D spheroid measurements
result: Close correlation between simulated and in vitro viability data

problem: Forming virtual cells in manufacturing considering real-life production factors.
method: Flexible clustering using Kohonen self-organising map for virtual cell formation.
dataset: Manufacturing production data

problem: Handling complex urban traffic behavior such as signalized intersections in cell transmission models.
method: Extended Urban Cell Transmission Model (UCTM) using agent-based concepts for turning, traffic signal, and lane-changing behavior modeling.
finding: The model replicates periodical queue generation/dissipation at signalized intersections and overall urban traffic phenomena adequately.
dataset: NGSIM arterial dataset
metric: Traffic flow replication accuracy
result: Adequately replicates urban traffic phenomena (validated with NGSIM)

problem: Understanding the role of regulatory T cells (Tregs) in preventing or slowing Type 1 Diabetes progression.
method: 2D agent-based model simulating immune response in mice, testing effects of Tregs from different sources and with different functions on tissue protection.
finding: A small number of Tregs can effectively protect islets by inhibiting effector T cell proliferation via DCs; Tregs added mid-response inhibit effector CD8+ T cell production and maintain β cell regeneration environment.
dataset: Simulated mouse pancreatic tissue immune response
metric: Islet protection, effector T cell proliferation inhibition
result: Small number of Tregs effectively protects islets

problem: Understanding smooth muscle cell migration induced by mechanical vibration.
method: Agent-based modelling of smooth muscle cell migration under mechanical vibration.
dataset: Smooth muscle cells

problem: Determining the relative contribution of free virus versus cell-to-cell transmission to hepatitis C virus dissemination in vitro.
method: Agent-based model explicitly incorporating distal (free-virus) and local (cell-to-cell) infection modes, validated with in vitro HCV data with and without neutralizing antibodies.
finding: Cell-to-cell infection accounts for 99% of infection events, yet blocking free-virus route causes 57% reduction at 72 hpi because free-virus provides access to distant uninfected areas enhancing cell-to-cell spread.
dataset: In vitro HCV infections (with/without free-virus neutralizing antibodies)
metric: Percentage of infection events by route, infection event reduction
result: Cell-to-cell: 99% (84–100% CI) of infections; free-virus block: 57% reduction at 72 hpi

problem: Generating whole-cell models requires high-resolution imaging of cellular ultrastructure.
method: Soft X-ray tomography for 3D imaging of whole cells to generate structural data for whole-cell models.
dataset: Whole cells (soft X-ray tomography)

problem: Understanding the effects of priming with whole-cell Bordetella pertussis vaccine on immunity.
method: Mathematical modeling of whole-cell pertussis vaccine priming effects.
dataset: Bordetella pertussis vaccination data

problem: Investigating whether the assumption that 28 independent modules can be simulated with a 1-second time step is valid for the M. genitalium whole-cell model.
method: Simulation of M. genitalium cell cycle using various simulation time step sizes, comparing results to the standard 1-second step.
dataset: Mycoplasma genitalium whole-cell model (28 modules)
metric: Cell cycle simulation results vs step size

problem: Developing a fiber-optic biosensor with bioluminescent whole-cell bioreporters for remote in-situ detection of BTEX compounds.
method: Mathematical model of optical element shape based on geometric optics, with MATLAB numerical simulation of ray transmission for different OE shapes; Pseudomonas putida TVA8 as bioreporter.
finding: Biosensor sensitivity depends on detected bioluminescence level, number of immobilized cells, and optical element transmission properties.
dataset: Pseudomonas putida TVA8 bioreporter; BTEX compounds (benzene, toluene, ethylbenzene, xylenes)
metric: Bioluminescence signal (~500 nm), ray transmission efficiency

problem: Whole-cell model simulations using stochastic methods are extremely time-consuming for the largest known biochemical reaction networks.
method: Parallel implementation of stochastic simulation algorithm (SSA) applied to a whole-cell reaction network.
finding: Parallel SSA implementation accelerates whole-cell model simulation development.
dataset: Whole-cell reaction network
metric: Simulation speed

problem: Implications of material-level improvements on lithium-ion battery cell performance are hard to quantify, hindering accelerated battery development.
method: Bottom-up battery cell design model calculating performance parameters from design parameters and material characteristics in a functional database structure from material to cell level.
finding: The model provides accurate estimations for cell weight, capacity, and energy density, validated on two different battery cells.
dataset: Two lithium-ion battery cells (validation)
metric: Weight, capacity, energy density estimation accuracy
result: Accurate estimations for weight, capacity, and energy density

problem: Whether AI can build a virtual cell by modeling life's smallest unit.
Computing osmotic pressure of dilute electrolyte solutions with charged macro-ions, accounting for shape anisotropy and surface charge distribution beyond mean-field approximations.
method: Extended virtual cell model combining coarse-grained Monte Carlo simulations (one-body and two-body) with all-atom molecular dynamics, using spherical virtual subspace for protein number density.
finding: Protein concentration-dependent osmotic pressures can be obtained by confining counterions in a virtual spherical subspace; specific interaction parameters enable study of ion-specific effects with a single protein molecule.
dataset: Charged biomolecules (proteins) in electrolyte solutions
metric: Osmotic pressure
result: Validated upper limit for neglecting macro-ion correlations; extended to all-atom MD

problem: Studying morphological variation in endothelial cell populations using computational modeling.
method: Virtual-cell model of endothelial cell morphological variation.
dataset: Endothelial cells

problem: Existing GRN inference methods rely on static models that fail to capture dynamic regulatory changes during cell differentiation, limiting virtual cell simulation.
method: CellProphet: interpretable AI model integrating temporal causality with transformer self-attention to infer dynamic gene regulatory networks with time-lagged TF-target dependencies.
finding: CellProphet achieves superior performance across all evaluation metrics on 7 differentiation datasets, identifies known and novel TFs, and accurately predicts cell fate transitions following in silico perturbation of Gata1 and Spi1.
dataset: 7 differentiation datasets; mouse embryonic stem cells; mouse hematopoietic differentiation
metric: GRN inference evaluation metrics (all), TF sensitivity
result: Superior performance in all evaluation metrics across 7 datasets

problem: Extensive crosstalk between programmed cell death pathways (apoptosis, pyroptosis, necroptosis) creates redundancies that challenge therapeutic control of cytokine storm.
method: Agent-based model (PCDABM) in NetLogo simulating activation of PCDPs by IAV, EPEC, and Salmonella enterica, with silencing experiments and simulated anti-TNF/anti-IL-1 therapies.
finding: PCDP redundancies maintain protection against diverse pathogens but challenge immunomodulation; anti-TNF and anti-IL-1 simulations did not reduce aggregate inflammation-generated system damage.
dataset: Simulated infections: influenza A virus (IAV), enteropathic E. coli (EPEC), Salmonella enterica (SE)
metric: Microbial clearance, aggregated inflammation-generated system damage
baseline: No PCDP silencing; no therapeutic intervention
result: Anti-TNF and anti-IL-1 did not reduce aggregate system damage

problem: Evaluating cost-effectiveness in the manufacture of allogeneic human induced pluripotent cells.
method: Agent-based model for cost-effectiveness analysis of hiPSC manufacturing in Japan.
dataset: Allogeneic human iPSC manufacturing (Japan)

problem: Designing steroid-based hydrazones as potential anabolic agents with computational and biological validation.
method: Design, synthesis, computational studies, and biological evaluation of steroid-based hydrazones.
dataset: Human skeletal muscle cell line

problem: Agent-based models with deformable membranes in 3D environments face computational bottlenecks in how agents perceive their surroundings.
method: GPU algorithm (OpenCL/CUDA) enabling agents to integrate signals scattered on their virtual membranes in 3D agent-based simulations.
finding: The GPU algorithm provides significant speed improvements for membrane signal integration in current GPU devices.
dataset: Virtual cells with deformable membranes (up to millions of agents)
metric: Processing speed, scalability

problem: Existing cell simulations lack fidelity and real-time interactivity for illustrating structures and processes within biological cells.
method: Eukaryo: 3D interactive agent-based simulation of a eukaryotic cell in a game engine with visual scripting, combining agent-based and mathematical modeling in VR.
finding: Eukaryo offers superior representations of cellular architecture, components, and dynamics compared to traditional media like illustrations and videos.
dataset: Eukaryotic cell (gene expression, energy production, waste removal)
result: Superior representations of cellular architecture and dynamics

problem: Modeling the budding yeast cell cycle regulatory network.
method: Agent-based model of the budding yeast cell cycle regulatory network.
dataset: Budding yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae)

problem: Building whole-cell computational models to predict cellular phenotypes and accelerate biological discovery.
Assembling the large and diverse array of data needed for whole-cell modeling remains a major challenge due to scattered data organization.
method: Review and analysis of data centralization strategies, proposing a central data warehouse for whole-cell modeling.
finding: A central data warehouse can overcome challenges of scattered data to enable more predictive whole-cell models, requiring community collaboration.

problem: Estimating half-cell thermodynamic parameters from whole-cell Li-ion battery experiments with low error.
method: Multi-species, multi-reaction (MSMR) half-cell thermodynamic model adapted to whole-cell cycling, with sequential least-square optimization and bootstrap analysis.
finding: Low-error fits achieved for open-circuit voltage and differential voltage curves; identified specific MSMR insertion reactions responsible for capacity loss across 600 cell cycles.
dataset: Samsung 18650 NMC|C cells, 600 charge-discharge cycles
metric: Mean absolute error (MAE)
baseline: Literature reference MSMR half-cell parameters (1-DOF fit)
result: Under 5 mV MAE for charge/discharge curves

problem: Integrated whole-cell geometric modeling of organelle interactions in S. cerevisiae.
dataset: S. cerevisiae

problem: Estimating half-cell thermodynamic parameters from whole-cell Li-ion battery experiments with low error.
method: Multi-species, multi-reaction (MSMR) half-cell thermodynamic model adapted to whole-cell cycling, with sequential least-square optimization and bootstrap analysis.
finding: Low-error fits achieved for open-circuit voltage and differential voltage curves; identified specific MSMR insertion reactions responsible for capacity loss across 600 cell cycles.
dataset: Samsung 18650 NMC|C cells, 600 charge-discharge cycles
metric: Mean absolute error (MAE)
baseline: Literature reference MSMR half-cell parameters (1-DOF fit)
result: Under 5 mV MAE for charge/discharge curves

problem: No models of E. coli peptidoglycan (PG) have been constructed on the whole-cell scale with full compositional heterogeneity.
method: Multi-scale coarse-grained modeling (one CG bead per glycan), stochastic simulation of cross-linking, and PG_maker code for whole-cell PG construction.
finding: Built whole-cell PG models consistent with experimental data; pore size distributions showed surprisingly good agreement with experiments; analyzed effects of Braun's lipoprotein on periplasmic protein diffusion.
dataset: Escherichia coli peptidoglycan layer
metric: Pore size distribution agreement with experimental estimates
result: Whole-cell PG models built in under an hour; pore sizes in good agreement with experimental estimates

problem: Investigating the impact of gene expression strategies in near whole-cell models of minimal bacteria.
dataset: Minimal bacteria

problem: Whole-cell models of bacteria lack replication of genetic material, the most fundamental process of life.
method: Brownian dynamics polymer simulation of replicating chromosomes at 10 bp resolution, with loop extrusion by SMC complexes and topoisomerase action, augmented by cryo-electron tomogram-derived ribosome distributions.
finding: Extended an existing whole-cell model of JCVI-syn3A to cover the entire cell cycle by modeling chromosome replication, organization changes, and multi-fork replication states.
dataset: JCVI-syn3A (genetically minimal bacterium)

problem: Establishing an ex vivo tool to quantify lens epithelial cell viability on whole porcine lenses for cytotoxicity studies.
method: Triple Hoechst-Ethidium-Calcein-AM (HEC) staining on whole porcine lenses with Staurosporine (STS) chemical toxicity model; macroscope imaging and ImageJ analysis.
finding: Triple HEC staining detected subtle mortality differences on whole lenses, confirming sensitivity for cytotoxicity studies of therapeutics targeting the lens.
dataset: 10 pairs of 6-month-old porcine lenses
metric: Viable epithelial cell density (vEpCD, cells/mm²)
result: STS: 3804 cells/mm² vs control: 3896 cells/mm², p=0.002

problem: Establishing an ex vivo tool to quantify lens epithelial cell viability on whole porcine lenses for cytotoxicity studies.
method: Triple Hoechst-Ethidium-Calcein-AM (HEC) staining on whole porcine lenses with Staurosporine (STS) chemical toxicity model; macroscope imaging and ImageJ analysis.
finding: Triple HEC staining detected subtle mortality differences on whole lenses, confirming sensitivity for cytotoxicity studies of therapeutics targeting the lens.
dataset: 10 pairs of 6-month-old porcine lenses
metric: Viable epithelial cell density (vEpCD, cells/mm²)
result: STS: 3804 cells/mm² vs control: 3896 cells/mm², p=0.002

problem: Determining thermodynamically accurate kinetic parameters for whole-cell reaction models.
Developing a hybrid stochastic/deterministic whole-cell model of the minimal cell JCVI-syn3A.
dataset: JCVI-syn3A (minimal cell)

problem: Investigating region- and cell-specific imbalances in inhibitory neurons in a mouse model for Kleefstra Syndrome using whole-brain clearing.
dataset: Mouse model for Kleefstra Syndrome

problem: Current antibody discovery methods lack the scale to fully screen the antibody repertoire of transgenic mouse models.
method: Novel combinatorial barcoding approach for high-throughput single cell BCR and whole transcriptome profiling of transgenic mouse samples.
finding: Profiled over 1 million cells obtaining hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes; detected all B cell subpopulations at single cell resolution; found specific VDJ utilization and amino acid motif patterns in expanded clonotypes.
dataset: Multiple transgenic mice (naive and immunized), >1 million cells
metric: Paired BCR clonotype count, B cell subpopulation detection
result: >1 million cells profiled, hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes

problem: Current antibody discovery methods lack the scale to fully screen the antibody repertoire of transgenic mouse models.
method: Novel combinatorial barcoding approach for high-throughput single cell BCR and whole transcriptome profiling of transgenic mouse samples.
finding: Profiled over 1 million cells obtaining hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes; detected all B cell subpopulations at single cell resolution; found specific VDJ utilization patterns in expanded clonotypes.
dataset: Multiple transgenic mice (naive and immunized), >1 million cells
metric: Paired BCR clonotype count, B cell subpopulation detection
result: >1 million cells profiled, hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes

problem: Mechanisms driving clonal dominance and ineffective hematopoiesis in PNH with bone marrow failure remain unclear; no PNH-specific mutations have been conclusively identified.
method: Patient-derived iPSC modeling (PNH-iPSCs and matched wild-type N-iPSCs), CRISPR/Cas9 PIGA correction, hematopoietic differentiation, and whole-genome sequencing (WGS).
finding: PIGA correction failed to fully restore normal hematopoiesis; WGS identified PNH clone-specific somatic mutations (including DNMT3A, ASXL1 and novel genes) in all cases, supporting genetic heterogeneity of PNH-BMF.
dataset: 5 PNH patients with bone marrow failure, matched iPSC lines
metric: Hematopoietic differentiation/proliferation capacity, somatic mutation identification
baseline: Matched wild-type N-iPSCs from same patients
result: Multiple PNH clone-specific somatic mutations identified in all 4 sequenced patients; one patient progressed to MDS

problem: Translating in vitro buccal permeation data to in vivo and whole-body exposure predictions.
Replacing expensive induction-loop traffic sensors with cell phone-based sensors while overcoming public privacy opt-in barriers.
method: Cell phone radio signal detection as virtual sensors for traffic light controllers, with careful controller training to work with low opt-in rates.
finding: Even with a small percentage of opted-in users, the new traffic controllers provide large benefits to all drivers.
dataset: Simulated traffic scenarios
metric: Traffic flow improvement, driver benefit
result: Large benefits with small percentage of opted-in users

problem: Making rule-based modeling and simulation accessible to beginners through an intuitive graphical interface within Virtual Cell.
Modeling the effect of UBE3A on dendritic spine morphogenesis using rule-based modeling.
Efficient handover measurement in small-cell networks to reduce processing time and overhead by distinguishing intra-cell and inter-cell handovers.
method: Virtual cell synchronization signal (VCSS) with hierarchical macro-cell ID and virtual-cell ID using 3D beamforming in LTE-based small-cell networks.
finding: The proposed VCSS-based technique significantly reduces handover processing time and overhead by distinguishing between intra-cell and inter-cell handovers.
dataset: Two LTE-based small-cell network deployment scenarios (simulation)
metric: Handover processing time, overhead reduction
result: Significant reduction in processing time and overhead

problem: Extending rule-based modeling to the spatial domain with Virtual Cell (VCELL).
Isolating CTC clusters from background leukocytes without shear stress damage, which existing physical-based methods cannot avoid.
method: Optically-induced-dielectrophoresis (ODEP)-based virtual cell microfilters in a T-shaped microfluidic chip, using controllable light patterns as virtual electrodes.
finding: Established a high-purity CTC cluster isolation method with 70.1% recovery and 60.8% purity, avoiding shear stress damage to isolated clusters.
dataset: CTC clusters (2-13 cells) mixed with background leukocytes
metric: Recovery rate, purity
result: Recovery 70.1±7.1%, purity 60.8±2.7%

problem: Cell biophysics modeling and simulation with Virtual Cell software.
In ultra-dense networks, overlapping user-centric virtual cells make multi-dimensional cooperation difficult due to complex interference and coupled resource dimensions.
method: Graph-based virtual cell forming with Pairwise Maximum Weights (PMW) clustering, using edge weight design capturing interference-fair power allocation, MU-MIMO, and cooperative transmission.
finding: The proposed algorithm effectively increases system performance at low complexity compared to reference schemes.
dataset: Simulated ultra-dense 5G network
metric: System performance (throughput), complexity
result: Improved system performance at low complexity

problem: Predicting binding and distribution of chemicals in in vitro toxicity assays using virtual cell models.
Traditional iterative search water filling (ISWF) for power allocation in ultra-dense networks requires too many iterations.
method: Low-complexity iterative water filling (LAWF) and sorting water filling (SWF) algorithms for virtual cell power allocation in UDN.
finding: LAWF and SWF achieve better sum rates than ISWF with fewer iterations; SWF performance increases with UDN deployment density.
dataset: Simulated ultra-dense network
metric: Sum rate
result: LAWF and SWF outperform ISWF in sum rate

problem: Virtual cell power allocation and interference management in ultra-dense networks.
Virtual cell formation for cellular manufacturing systems considering real-life production constraints to improve scheduling flexibility.
method: Fuzzy c-means clustering approach for machine cell formation using production attributes (processing time, alternative routings, sequence of operation, machine flexibility, capacity, product demand).
finding: The proposed fuzzy c-means method outperforms existing methods in many instances for cell formation across 15 varied-size problems from literature.
dataset: 15 cases of different size problems from open literature
metric: Group technology efficiency (GTE), exceptional elements (EE)
result: Better performance than existing methods in many instances

problem: Mitigating both intra-virtual-cell and inter-virtual-cell interference in uplink cooperative decoding systems.
method: Resource allocation technique using interference graphs between base stations at virtual cell edges, with frequency band allocation based on user counts per base station.
finding: The proposed scheme decreases the number of unsatisfied users whose rate falls below guaranteed bit rates in a mmWave system.
dataset: Simulated mmWave uplink virtual cell system
metric: Number of unsatisfied users (rate below guaranteed bit rate)
result: Fewer unsatisfied users at 128/256/512 kbps guaranteed rates

problem: Achieving high power density in power electronic transformers using multi-cell rectifier topologies.
method: ISOS multi-cell full-wave rectifier with GaN triangular current mode (TCM) virtual capacitor converters for voltage sharing without sensors or feedback control.
finding: Prototype demonstrates potential for over 96% efficiency and over 3.0 W/cm³ power density, enabling high power density power electronic transformers.
dataset: AC 100V, 450W prototype with 3 cell converters and 2 VCCs
metric: Efficiency, power density (W/cm³)
result: Efficiency >96.0%, power density >3.0 W/cm³

problem: Building a virtual work environment to support design and planning of human-robot collaborative assembly cells.
method: VR interface-based motion simulation in virtual environment for interactive evaluation and improvement of assembly process plans and equipment layouts.
dataset: Human-robot collaborative assembly scenarios

problem: Isolating CTC clusters from background leukocytes without shear stress damage, which existing physical-based methods cannot avoid.
method: Optically-induced-dielectrophoresis (ODEP)-based virtual cell microfilters in a T-shaped microfluidic chip, using controllable light patterns as virtual electrodes.
finding: Established a high-purity CTC cluster isolation method with 70.1% recovery and 60.8% purity, avoiding shear stress damage to isolated clusters.
dataset: CTC clusters (2-13 cells) mixed with background leukocytes
metric: Recovery rate, purity
result: Recovery 70.1±7.1%, purity 60.8±2.7%

problem: Non-invasive virtual cell labeling by coupling single-cell innate fluorescent signatures with spatial information.
Frequent adjustment of virtual cell scheduling plans due to new order arrivals causes production instability.
method: Insertion rescheduling strategy based on slack time subdivision (idle, partial relaxation, total relaxation) solved by backtracking search algorithm with non-dominated sorting.
finding: The proposed rescheduling strategy minimizes maximum completion time and instability coefficient, verified through numerical example.
dataset: Numerical example (manufacturing scheduling)
metric: Maximum completion time, instability coefficient

problem: Virtual Cell modeling and simulation software development.
The concept and development of the virtual cell.
The concept and development of the virtual cell.
Traditional in-person cell culture labs face accessibility, cost, and infrastructure barriers; virtual alternatives lack decision-driven interactivity.
method: Interactive 3D browser-based virtual mammalian cell culture simulation with independent decision-making and technical feedback, assessed via qualitative surveys.
finding: 73% of students found clear real-world connections, 57% agreed it aided cell culture understanding, and over half found the experience realistic and requiring critical thinking.
dataset: Upper-level undergraduate/graduate biotechnology students (n=87+96 post-lab, n=73+74 end-of-course)
metric: Student perception survey responses (%)
result: 73% real-world connections, 57% improved understanding

problem: Computationally modeling the complex process of amoeboid cell crawling including cell polarization, shape deformation, and locomotion.
method: Phase field approach coupled with stochastic partial differential equations for intracellular polarization, enabling simultaneous calculation of shape deformations and cell locomotion.
finding: The phase field model successfully integrates interior polarization dynamics with cell shape changes and locomotion for modeling chemotaxis.

problem: Virtual Cell modeling and simulation environment development.
Energy-efficient data propagation in wireless sensor networks with a single mobile sink to extend network lifetime.
method: Virtual cell-based data dissemination with cluster heads aggregating data from member nodes and relaying to a mobile sink following a specific route pattern.
finding: The approach reduces energy consumption, minimizes packet loss, and enhances network lifetime compared to existing routing protocols.
dataset: Simulated wireless sensor network with single mobile sink
metric: Energy consumption, packet loss, network lifetime, data delivery speed
result: Decreased energy consumption, improved network lifetime and data delivery

problem: Generating large-scale Perturb-seq datasets to power virtual cell AI models.
dataset: Largest Perturb-Seq dataset (Xaira Therapeutics)

problem: Accelerating AI model development for virtual cell modeling.
Accelerating AI model development for virtual cell modeling.
Dendritic cells play a critical role in shaping immunity, but the complex multiscale molecular and cellular networks underlying DC-mediated immune responses are difficult to study experimentally.
method: Developed a logical and predictive multiscale model integrating DC population heterogeneity, APC function, and cell-cell interactions spanning molecular to population levels, with KEGG enrichment analysis.
finding: The model characterized DC responses to SARS-CoV-2/influenza co-infection, predicted DC-T cell crosstalk in cancer microenvironments, and identified 45 diseases and 24 molecular pathways addressable by the DC model.
dataset: Human dendritic cells, SARS-CoV-2/influenza infection, cancer microenvironment
result: 45 diseases and 24 molecular pathways identified

problem: The mechanism by which 8 µm red blood cells pass through 0.3 µm interendothelial slits in the spleen for filtration remains enigmatic, including whether slits must widen during passage.
method: Combined multiscale modeling, live imaging, and microfluidic experiments with slits of defined physiological dimensions including submicron width.
finding: Healthy RBCs pass through 0.28 µm rigid slits without slit opening, requiring sufficient surface area-to-volume ratio and spectrin cytoskeleton unfolding. Transit dynamics is controlled by cytoplasmic viscosity, scaling as 2D Poiseuille flow.
dataset: Human red blood cells, microfluidic slit devices (0.28 µm width)
metric: Transit time scaling with pressure drop and slit width
baseline: Previous literature proposing slit caliber increase during RBC passage
result: Transit time scales with pressure^-1 and slit width^-3

problem: Battery cell manufacturing requires improved multiscale and multiphysics modelling tools.
method: Multiscale and multiphysics modelling approaches for battery cell manufacturing.
dataset: Battery cells

problem: Soil deformation and strength exhibit size effects due to granular characteristics, requiring multi-scale characterisation of strain localisation phenomena.
method: Triaxial compression tests with different sizes of reinforcing particles combined with numerical simulation, using the soil cell element model theory and average strain energy release coefficient.
finding: Samples exhibit various failure patterns (single, x-shaped, v-shaped, parallel). The ratio of intrinsic scale to particle size decreases as reinforcing particle size increases, and localised strain zones activate before peak stress.
dataset: Soil samples with various reinforcing particle sizes
metric: Average strain energy release coefficient, intrinsic scale to particle size ratio
result: Size effects confirmed; non-elastic dissipation energy increases before stress peak

problem: Chondrocyte-articular cartilage model integration for studying osteoarthritis is not fully tackled, making it difficult to assess biological and mechanical interactions simultaneously.
method: Multi-scale model coupling 2D FEM of articular cartilage with regulatory network-based chondrocyte models via reaction-diffusion equations, validated with in vitro IL-1β cartilage degradation experiments.
finding: Predicted pro-inflammatory chondrocyte activity matched experimental tendencies after 2 weeks. Initial chondrocyte phenotypes persisted, consistent with difficulty of AC regeneration in OA. Discrepancies found for MMP3, IL-6, and ITGA5.
dataset: Bovine knee articular cartilage plugs (5mm, 3 calves, 2 replicates), IL-1β 10ng/mL treatment
metric: OA gene expression comparison (computational vs experimental)
result: Pro-inflammatory predictions matched experimental tendency at 2 weeks; discrepancies in MMP3, IL-6, ITGA5

problem: It remains unclear how mechanical, chemical, and biological factors interact to regulate transmembrane adhesion proteins and shape cross-scale adhesion dynamics from molecular clustering to cellular migration.
method: Multiscale mechanobiochemical coupling framework integrating key molecular steps in the integrin life cycle including activation, clustering, signal transduction, and internalization.
finding: Identified an antagonistic interplay between integrin internalization and clustering governing cross-scale adhesion dynamics. The model demonstrates how substrate stiffness regulates integrin clustering size and internalization rate, explaining the transition between durotaxis and negative durotaxis.
dataset: Cell-substrate adhesion system (integrin-ECM)
metric: Integrin clustering size, internalization rate
result: Quantitative demonstration of stiffness-dependent clustering and durotaxis transition

problem: Computational cell biology requires software tools addressing broad scientific needs for modeling biochemical and electrophysiological events distributed within complex cellular structures.
method: Developed the Virtual Cell software environment supporting compartmental and spatial models with idealized or experimentally derived 1D/2D/3D geometries, accessible via web browser.
finding: The Virtual Cell enables formulation of both compartmental and spatial cell biological models and generation of simulations, serving cell biologists, mathematical biologists, and bioengineers.

problem: Cell biological processes involve complex interacting biochemical and electrophysiological events requiring computational models for applying the scientific method.
method: The Virtual Cell computational environment for constructing quantitative models and generating predictive simulations, with applications to nucleocytoplasmic transport and intracellular calcium dynamics.
finding: Virtual Cell facilitates model assembly and demonstrates the power of computational modeling for elucidating cell biology through applications in nuclear transport and calcium signaling.
dataset: Nucleocytoplasmic transport, intracellular calcium dynamics

problem: Cell biology modeling requires integrated software supporting diverse molecular mechanisms, spatial geometries, and both deterministic and stochastic simulation approaches.
method: Virtual Cell (VCell) web-based client-server system integrating reaction kinetics, diffusion, flow, membrane transport, electrophysiology with experimental microscope image-derived geometries, supporting SBML/CellML/MatLab exchange.
finding: VCell provides a physically consistent, mathematically rigorous spatial modeling framework with over 1000 worldwide users, supporting both deterministic and stochastic algorithms including full PDE solvers for 3D geometries.
result: 1000+ worldwide users

problem: Designing beamformers for virtual cell networks is highly challenging due to coupled interference and desired signals under arbitrarily formed virtual cells.
method: Developed a new formulation of the beamforming problem for sum-rate maximization in virtual cell networks and analyzed optimal solution structure to create a balanced beamforming algorithm.
finding: The balanced beamforming algorithm mitigates edge user effect and outperforms existing algorithms by balancing desired signal maximization and interference minimization in overlapping virtual cells.
dataset: Cooperative cellular network simulations
metric: Sum-rate
result: Outperforms existing algorithms in sum-rate across various scenarios

problem: Cell biological modeling requires a computational framework for coupling reaction and diffusion on experimental geometries.
method: Computational framework mapping experimental biochemical and electrophysiological data onto experimental images for reaction-diffusion modeling.
finding: The framework enables construction of complex general models coupling reaction and diffusion in cell biology.

problem: Manufacturing environments require physically reconfigurable virtual cells to handle highly dynamic production conditions.
dataset: Manufacturing systems

problem: Modeling physicochemical and electrophysiological processes in living cells requires general computational tools supporting spatial and temporal simulations.
method: Virtual Cell (VCell) framework providing automated tools for deterministic and stochastic simulations, coupling electrophysiology and reaction kinetics with transport mechanisms on irregular 3D geometries from experimental images.
finding: VCell provides robust computational tools for spatially resolved cell biology models, supporting complex geometries and multiple simulation paradigms.

problem: Joint transmission from massive distributed antennas achieves rate gains but with unaffordable computational cost and channel measurement overhead; a scalable framework based on virtual cells is needed.
method: Analytical derivation of ergodic rate as a function of large-scale fading coefficients for MRT and ZFBF in virtual-cell-based distributed antenna systems, with optimization of virtual cell size.
finding: Without grouping, small virtual cell size is preferred; with user grouping via overlapped virtual cells, average user rate significantly improves with increasing virtual cell size, at higher signal processing cost.
dataset: Large-scale distributed antenna system simulations
metric: Average user rate, ergodic rate
result: Significant rate improvement with user grouping and larger virtual cells

problem: Traditional cellular manufacturing systems have fixed layouts unsuitable for frequent product mix changes; virtual cells offer logical grouping without physical reconfiguration.
method: Methodology for designing virtual manufacturing cells where machines belonging to the same cell need not occupy contiguous areas on the shop floor.
finding: Virtual cellular manufacturing systems combine the benefits of cellular manufacturing with the flexibility to handle changing product mixes without physical reconfiguration.
dataset: Manufacturing shop floor

problem: Understanding biological systems across spatial scales from genome to organ requires approaches that account for cellular heterogeneity and noise, which traditional top-down or bottom-up methods miss.
method: Review of cellular automata and agent-based modeling approaches where individual cells are represented as virtual entities governed by simple rules, integrated with PDE/ODE models for multi-scale phenomena.
finding: Individual-based virtual cell models enable direct mapping between real and virtual cells, are inherently extensible, and play an important role in developing 'middle-out' models for multi-scale biology.
baseline: Top-down and bottom-up systems biology approaches

problem: The number of mitochondria per cell and mitochondrial DNA content need quantification across mammalian cell types.
method: New biochemical method for estimating virtual number of mitochondria per cell, combined with plasmid probe to measure mtDNA per mitochondrion and per cell in five cell types from four mammalian species.
finding: MtDNA per mitochondrion is essentially constant across cell types (mean 2.6 molecules/mt), while mtDNA molecules per cell span an 8-fold range. Virtual mt number per cell ranges from 83 to 677.
dataset: 5 cell types from 4 mammalian species
metric: mtDNA molecules/mitochondrion, mtDNA molecules/cell, virtual mt number/cell
result: 2.6±0.30 mtDNA/mt; 220-1720 mtDNA/cell; 83-677 mt/cell

problem: Traditional cellular manufacturing lacks flexibility to respond to demand pattern changes, while job shops lack setup efficiency.
method: Virtual cellular manufacturing using scheduling mechanisms to form temporary logical cells within a process layout, evaluated by simulation comparing to traditional cellular and job shop approaches.
finding: Virtual cellular manufacturing yields significantly better shop performance over a range of operating conditions compared to traditional cellular and job shop approaches.
dataset: Manufacturing simulation
metric: Shop performance
result: Significantly better performance than both baselines

problem: V2X communications require user-centric virtual cells for broadcast groups of vehicles, where the virtual cell concept must be extended from single-user to moving hotspot scenarios.
method: Three-stage optimization approach for V2X virtual cells: admission control, transmission weight selection, and power control for broadcast groups.
finding: The proposed VC realization for V2X enables efficient broadcast to moving vehicle groups through optimized admission control and power management.
dataset: V2X communication scenarios

problem: Cellular network performance is limited by inter-cell interference, and characterizing the dependency of interference on positions of BS antennas and users remains elusive.
method: Comparative analytical study of uplink ergodic sum capacity for co-located vs distributed antenna layouts, deriving inter-cell interference density and sum capacity as functions of antenna configuration and virtual cell size.
finding: Distributed antenna layout reduces inter-cell interference density scaling to L_c^(-α/2) vs L_c^(-1) for co-located. Virtual cells achieve uniform interference density and enable local distributed signal processing.
dataset: Distributed antenna system simulations
metric: Uplink ergodic sum capacity, ergodic rate, inter-cell interference density
result: Interference scales as L_c^(-α/2) for DA vs L_c^(-1) for CA

problem: Modeling nonlinear cell-biophysical dynamics on arbitrarily complex geometries requires accessible computational tools for both biologists and theorists.
method: The Virtual Cell modeling tool with web-based GUI, server-side numerical algorithms, database storage, and spatial computations based on detailed mathematical formulations.
finding: The Virtual Cell enables specification and simulation of cell-biophysical models on arbitrary geometries, demonstrated through two typical modeling projects.

problem: Cell biological modeling requires frameworks for coupling reaction and diffusion using experimental biochemical data mapped onto experimental images.
method: Computational framework based on mapping experimental biochemical and electrophysiological data onto experimental images for reaction-diffusion modeling.
finding: The framework enables construction of complex general models for the class of problems coupling reaction and diffusion in cell biology.

problem: Development of a virtual cell (VICE) computational tool.
Multiple UAS operating in neighboring airspace face wireless communication conflicts requiring efficient frequency resource utilization via time-slot allocation.
method: Virtual cell based space partitioning for TDMA time-slot allocation with spatial reuse, considering UA mobility effects on network parameters.
finding: The proposed virtual cell based resource allocation enhances frequency utilization for unmanned aircraft systems in neighboring airspace.
dataset: UAS communication simulation
metric: Frequency resource utilization
result: Enhanced frequency utilization with spatial reuse

problem: In Cloud RAN, traditional cellular structure needs revamping to exploit unlimited RRH cooperation, requiring optimal user-centric virtual cell radius design.
method: Analytical characterization of interference distribution based on mean and variance for user-centric virtual cells with correlated RRH transmit powers, deriving optimal cell radius.
finding: Optimal virtual cell radius depends on user separation distance, RRH density, and other system parameters, providing guidelines for C-RAN cell planning in 5G networks.
dataset: Cloud RAN simulations
metric: System downlink capacity
result: Optimal cell radius derived as function of system parameters

problem: 3D multi-cell channel modeling with time evolution for enabling virtual field trials.
method: QuaDRiGa: 3D multi-cell channel model with time evolution.

problem: Modeling and simulation of cell biological dynamics requires a computational environment accessible to both experimental biologists and theoretical biophysicists.
method: Virtual Cell (VCell) computational environment supporting simple hypothesis-testing models to complex multilayered spatially resolved nonlinear systems.
finding: VCell enables a wide range of cell biology modeling applications, demonstrated through representative published examples from VCell users.

problem: Manufacturing cells facing changing production requirements need reconfiguration strategies that maintain performance targets.
method: Integrated framework based on multiple objective simulation optimization using Resource Elements (REs) to define processing capabilities of virtual cells for reconfiguration.
finding: Virtual (logical) cells serve as an effective reconfiguration strategy for manufacturing systems facing changing production requirements.
dataset: Manufacturing system simulation
metric: Overall system performance (multiple objectives)

problem: Cell biology experiments need computational support for model construction, simulation, and analysis incorporating realistic 3D experimental geometries.
method: Virtual Cell modular computational framework with JAVA web interface, supporting geometry from microscope images, compartment topology, species and reaction specification, and computational mesh generation.
finding: The Virtual Cell facilitates communication between cell biologists and mathematical biologists while supporting both experimental interpretation and planning.

problem: In 5G virtual cell networks, predicting the optimal set of base stations for mobile users becomes time-critical as mobility and data rate demands increase.
method: Recurrent Neural Network trained on sequences of Received Signal Strength (RSS) values to predict next base station association for virtual cell formation.
finding: The RNN-based method achieves over 98% accuracy in predicting optimal virtual cell topology within the time constraints imposed by user mobility.
dataset: Simulated 5G multi-cell RSS sequences
metric: Prediction accuracy
result: Accuracy >98%

problem: Evaluating the performance of virtual cells and multistage flow shops in manufacturing.
method: Analytical approach for performance evaluation.
dataset: Manufacturing systems

problem: Solving the dynamic virtual cell formation problem in manufacturing systems.
method: Linear programming embedded particle swarm optimization algorithm.
dataset: Manufacturing systems

problem: Systems biology requires quantitative and predictive whole-cell models to provide fundamental new insights into cellular behavior.
method: Commentary on Karr et al.'s first integrated and dynamic computational model of a bacterium accounting for all components and their interactions.
finding: The first whole-cell computational model represents a milestone for virtual cell biology, integrating all known molecular components and interactions of a bacterium.
dataset: Mycoplasma genitalium (whole bacterium)

problem: In dynamic manufacturing environments, cell formation becomes ineffective across periods and reconfiguration is costly; simultaneous consideration of supply chain and cell formation is needed.
method: Bi-objective possibilistic optimization model for integrated procurement, production, and distribution with virtual cell formation, solved using revised multi-choice goal programming.
finding: The integrated model with fuzzy parameters achieves lower distribution and procurement costs and faster customer response through virtual cell formation without reconfiguration charges.
dataset: Real-world industrial case
metric: Distribution cost, procurement cost, response time
result: Lower costs and faster response demonstrated on industrial case

problem: The concept and design of virtual manufacturing cells.
dataset: Manufacturing systems

problem: Virtual cell formation needs to minimize throughput time while handling routing flexibility and intercellular flow in manufacturing.
