method: Double-sweep algorithm for k-shortest path problem applied to virtual cell formation, with heuristic scheduling for multiple job orders.
finding: The method provides optimal virtual cell candidates with shortest throughput time plus sub-optimal alternatives, handling bottleneck machines shared across cells.
dataset: Manufacturing shop floor examples
metric: Throughput time
result: Optimal and sub-optimal route candidates with throughput times

problem: Mobility management in ultra-dense networks with virtual cells faces challenges from frequent handovers and massive signaling overhead, especially for high-speed vehicles.
method: Proactive mobility management using LSTM-Dead Reckoning trajectory prediction framework with SDN controller, active gNB selection for virtual cell formation based on predicted locations.
finding: The LSTM-DR prediction framework achieves higher accuracy and robustness; the proactive solution significantly reduces handover frequency, failure rate, and signaling overhead.
dataset: Real-world vehicle mobility dataset
metric: Prediction accuracy, handover frequency, handover failure rate, signaling overhead
result: Significant reduction in handover frequency and failure rate

problem: Constitutively active Stat3 in breast cancer promotes oncogenesis through cell proliferation, angiogenesis, and apoptosis resistance, making it an attractive therapeutic target.
method: Virtual database screening of ~429,000 compounds followed by cell-based functional assays including Stat3-dependent luciferase reporter, DNA binding, and dimerization assays.
finding: STA-21 was identified as a Stat3 inhibitor that blocks DNA binding, dimerization, and luciferase activity, and selectively reduces survival of breast cancer cells with constitutive Stat3 signaling.
dataset: Human breast cancer cell lines, ~429,000 compound library
metric: Stat3 DNA binding activity, luciferase activity, cell survival
result: Top compound from 429,000 screened; selective killing of Stat3-active cells

problem: Evaluating user-centric joint transmission performance in ultra-dense networks with virtual cells under realistic power constraints.
method: Stochastic geometry analysis of user-centric JT with MRT precoding under total transmit power constraint per user, resolving correlated transmit powers to derive average throughput.
finding: User-centric JT with MRT provides high SNR without severe interference to co-channel users. MRT precoding requiring CSI is essential for successful joint transmission.
dataset: Ultra-dense network simulations
metric: Average user throughput
result: High SNR with low inter-user interference

problem: Analyzing and comparing the behaviors of virtual cells versus traditional manufacturing systems.
method: Ant colony optimization (ACO)-based metamodels for behavior analysis.
dataset: Manufacturing systems

problem: Complex geometry grid generation for CFD requires simple and flexible methods that can handle arbitrary bodies.
method: Virtual cell embedding (VCE) method using structured rectilinear meshes with partial cell faces and volumes for complex geometries.
finding: VCE can generate grids for any body or multiple bodies, demonstrated on arc airfoils, ship airwake, and porous media flow, enabling rapid geometry modifications in the design process.
dataset: Arc airfoil, ship airwake, porous media geometries
metric: Accuracy, convergence
result: Demonstrated on multiple complex geometry applications

problem: Direct fully electromagnetic simulation of relativistic laser-plasma interactions requires 3D particle-in-cell codes.
method: 3D electromagnetic relativistic PIC code VLPL (Virtual Laser Plasma Lab) for simulating laser-plasma interactions including relativistic self-focusing.
finding: Background plasma electrons are accelerated to multi-MeV energies and 10^4 T magnetic fields are generated during self-focusing at high laser intensities, relevant to fast ignitor concept in inertial confinement fusion.
dataset: Under-dense plasma, relativistic laser pulse
metric: Electron energy, magnetic field strength
result: Multi-MeV electron energies, 10^4 T magnetic fields

problem: Virtual cell formation for agile manufacturing requires a multi-stage methodology.
method: Multi-stage methodology for virtual cell formation.
dataset: Manufacturing systems

problem: Teaching mathematical modeling in cell biology requires accessible computational environments and structured educational materials.
method: Lecture notes, slides, and student assignments using the Virtual Cell environment for teaching mathematical modeling.
finding: The Virtual Cell provides an effective platform for teaching mathematical modeling strategies in cell biology.

problem: Edge UE experience degradation and load imbalance in virtual radio access networks with cooperative transmit points.
method: UE-centric metric-based network partitioning into virtual TP sets with soft UE-TP association for load balancing. Numerical simulations.
finding: The proposed technique eliminates edge UE experience and soft association facilitates load-balancing among TPs.
dataset: simulated multi-cell wireless network
metric: UE gain, load balance
baseline: conventional VTP partitioning without soft association

problem: Need for a computational framework that enables cell biologists to construct spatial models incorporating realistic experimental geometries.
method: Modular computational framework (Virtual Cell) with Java web interface, 3D spatial modeling, numerical solvers, and geometry from microscope images.
finding: The Virtual Cell facilitates communication between cell biologists and mathematical biologists and aids interpretation and planning of experiments.
dataset: cell biological problems (various)

problem: Understanding the initiation and propagation of ectopic excitation in cardiac ventricular cells and tissues.
method: Computational models of human ventricular cells simulating after-depolarizations and up/down regulation of membrane conductance systems in homogeneous/heterogeneous tissues.
finding: Ectopic foci require sufficient size and coupling to drive surrounding tissue; propagation depends on tissue heterogeneity and anisotropy.
dataset: human ventricular cell models (isotropic, anisotropic, orthotropic tissues)

problem: Design of virtual manufacturing cells using optimization methods.
method: Mathematical programming approach.

problem: The mechanism behind age-related decline in primary CD8+ T cell responses and the role of virtual memory (TVM) cells.
method: Proliferation assays in aged mice and humans, adoptive transfer experiments, and molecular profiling of TVM cells.
finding: TVM cells become senescent with aging and are poorly proliferative in aged mice and humans, while conventional naive T cells retain proliferative capacity. This marks the first observation of senescence in an antigenically naive T cell population.
dataset: aged mice and human CD8+ T cells
metric: proliferative capacity
baseline: conventional naive T cells (TN), young TVM cells
result: TVM poorly proliferative in aged; TN retain capacity

problem: Fairness of resource management for V2X multicast communication in user-centric virtual cell ultra-dense networks with vehicle mobility.
method: Dynamic user-centric virtual cell (DUVC) scheme with mobile tracking and approximation algorithm for max-min-fair resource management.
finding: The proposed DUVC scheme is suitable for V2X communication and the algorithm outperforms three existing algorithms in fairness.
dataset: simulated 5G UDN V2X scenario
metric: fairness of resource management
result: outperforms all three baselines in fairness

problem: Joint dynamic AP clustering and precoding in user-centric virtual cell networks under time-varying channel conditions.
method: Two-stage approach: discrete particle swarm optimization for AP clustering exploiting channel correlation, then distributed WMMSE-based precoding.
finding: The DAPC-Pre algorithm converges fast and significantly outperforms benchmark algorithms in sum spectral efficiency under dynamic environments.
dataset: simulated user-centric virtual cell network with random walk mobility
metric: weighted sum spectral efficiency (SE)
baseline: benchmark AP clustering and precoding algorithms
result: significantly outperforms benchmarks in sum SE

problem: Frequent handover failures and ping-pong effects in ultra-dense networks due to dense small cell deployment.
method: Virtual cell-based mobility enhancement with optimized handover parameters (TTT, A3 Offset) and VC forming/reforming based on user direction and speed.
finding: System HOF rate is minimized by optimizing handover parameters; PP rate decreases more than 50% for moderate-speed users compared to LTE.
dataset: simulated 5G UDN
metric: handover failure (HOF) rate, ping-pong (PP) rate, SINR
result: PP rate decrease >50% vs LTE

problem: Distributed resource allocation (sub-channel, modulation, power control) in multi-cell OFDMA networks where pure non-cooperative games yield poor outcomes.
method: Non-cooperative game with a virtual referee that monitors and improves competition outcomes by adjusting rates or restricting sub-channel access.
finding: The virtual referee reduces transmitted power by 80% vs fixed channel assignment and 25% vs iterative water-filling, with 10% rate improvement and up to 40% power reduction in multi-cell severe interference.
dataset: simulated multi-cell OFDMA network (two-cell and multi-cell)
metric: transmitted power, achievable rate
result: 80% power reduction vs fixed, 25% vs water-filling, 10% rate improvement

problem: Production planning in virtual cells of reconfigurable manufacturing systems.
method: Genetic algorithm.

problem: Building generative statistical models of subcellular organization from microscopy images to learn possible phenotypes and inform simulations.
method: Automated computational methods for constructing generative statistical models of cellular/nuclear shapes and subcellular structure arrangement from image data.
finding: These models enable consistent phenotype analysis, discrimination, and can provide realistic geometry and initial protein locations for simulations of cellular processes.
dataset: microscopy images of cells

problem: No standard mechanism exists for using BioPAX pathway data from Pathway Commons to annotate and build quantitative mathematical models.
method: New Virtual Cell module for pathway data retrieval, visualization, and automatic creation of executable network models from qualitative pathway connections.
finding: The module enables direct integration of Pathway Commons data into Virtual Cell for quantitative modeling.
dataset: Pathway Commons (BioPAX pathway databases)

problem: Understanding when, where, and how virtual memory (VM) CD8 T cells are generated and maintained during normal development.
method: Analysis of VM cell populations in IL-4R-deficient animals, tracking VM appearance in periphery vs thymus, and assessment of basal proliferation and long-term maintenance.
finding: VM population size is reduced in IL-4R-deficient animals. VM cells appear first in the periphery (not thymus), show basal proliferation, and are durably maintained, including during unrelated infections.
dataset: mouse CD8 T cells (IL-4R-deficient and normal mice)
metric: VM population size, proliferation
result: reduced VM in IL-4R-deficient; VM appears first in periphery

problem: Inefficient resource utilization and pseudo congestion in TD-LTE systems where users in overlapping coverage cannot use resources from multiple base stations.
method: Virtual cell concept enabling users to utilize resources from multiple base stations with adaptive sub-frame allocation and cross-slot interference management.
finding: Virtual cells resolve pseudo congestion, enable customized user-specific resource utilization, and demonstrate performance enhancement over conventional TD-LTE.
dataset: simulated TD-LTE system
metric: resource utilization, throughput
result: performance enhancement vs conventional TD-LTE

problem: Forming virtual manufacturing cells that integrate machine grouping and layout design.
method: Four-phase approach: primary cell formation by operation range division, cell redesign for minimizing intercell travels, sub-cell decomposition, and forced decomposition for multiple designs.
finding: The methodology effectively forms virtual cells with minimized intercell travels and addresses the machine duplication problem.
dataset: illustrative manufacturing example
metric: number of intercell travels

problem: Frequent handover failures and high signaling overhead due to dense small cell deployment in ultra-dense networks.
method: Virtual cell design with local anchor cell for intra/inter-VC handover, virtual PCI, virtual reference signals, and split bearers.
finding: Spectral efficiency gains up to 11% and handover failure reduction by more than 50% compared to traditional LTE.
dataset: simulated 5G UDN
metric: spectral efficiency, handover failure rate
result: 11% SE gain, >50% HOF reduction vs LTE

problem: The antigen-specific function of virtual memory (VM) CD8 T cells is poorly characterized and their contribution to pathogen responses is unclear.
method: Functional characterization of polyclonal VM cells including IFN-γ synthesis, differentiation phenotype, and Listeria monocytogenes infection control experiments.
finding: VM cells have unique functional properties distinct from naïve or antigen-primed memory cells: poor TCR-induced IFN-γ but efficient Listeria control, bridging innate and adaptive immunity.
dataset: mouse polyclonal VM CD8 T cells, Listeria monocytogenes infection model
metric: IFN-γ synthesis, pathogen clearance
baseline: naïve and antigen-primed conventional memory CD8 T cells
result: poor IFN-γ but efficient Listeria control

problem: Building AI-powered virtual cells using data-driven and closed-loop learning approaches.
Scheduling algorithm design for virtual cells.
Enhancing QoE in TD-LTE pico-cell hotspots where pseudo-congestion degrades user experience.
method: SDN-based framework with virtual cells enabling multi-base-station resource utilization, QoE-centric MOS monitoring, and centralized TD-LTE frame optimization.
finding: The framework leads to notable improvements in MOS, end-to-end delay, and packet loss rate compared to static and adaptive frame reconfiguration approaches.
dataset: simulated TD-LTE picocell system
metric: Mean Opinion Score (MOS), end-to-end delay, packet loss rate
baseline: static and adaptive frame reconfiguration in TD-LTE picocells
result: notable improvement in MOS, delay, and packet loss

problem: Functional job shops and classical cellular manufacturing systems perform poorly in volatile manufacturing environments; virtual cells need better layout strategies.
method: Novel capability-based distributed layout approach with simulated annealing heuristic algorithm for virtual cellular manufacturing.
finding: Capability-based distributed layout outperforms functional layouts for forming virtual manufacturing cells in dynamic environments.
dataset: real manufacturing data problem
metric: layout performance for virtual cell formation
result: distributed layout superior to functional layout

problem: Resource allocation and energy efficiency in IoT low-power wide-area networks (LoRaWAN) with massive heterogeneous devices.
method: Multilayer virtual cell-based spatial time division multiple access (STDMA) scheme with flexible spreading factor allocation and power consumption optimization.
finding: The proposed method simplifies interference calculation, enables flexible cell radius adjustment, and significantly improves data rate and energy efficiency over conventional methods.
dataset: simulated LoRaWAN IoT network
metric: power consumption, network data rate
result: significant improvement vs conventional method

problem: The mechanism behind the dramatic increase of memory phenotype CD8 T cells with aging is unknown; the prevailing hypothesis of lifelong antigenic stimulation lacks evidence.
method: Phenotypic analysis and functional studies in CD4-deficient, CCR5-deficient, and CXCR3-deficient aged mice to distinguish antigen-driven vs virtual memory CD8 T cell accumulation.
finding: Central memory CD8 T cells in aged mice are virtual memory cells that develop without antigenic stimulation, independent of CD4 T cells, CCR5, and CXCR3.
dataset: aged mouse CD8 T cells (CD4-KO, CCR5-KO, CXCR3-KO)
metric: central memory CD8 T cell proportion
result: accumulation independent of CD4, CCR5, CXCR3

problem: Handoff decision in virtual cell layout-based tactical communications systems requires considering multiple criteria beyond signal strength.
method: Multicriteria handoff decision algorithm (MDA) using fuzzy inference system processing signal strength, soft capacity, relative direction and speed of access points.
finding: MDA with lowest membership threshold achieves minimum handoffs; MDA with highest threshold achieves minimum call blocking rate, both at acceptable levels.
dataset: simulated virtual cell tactical communications
metric: number of handoffs, call blocking rate (GoS)
result: minimum handoffs or minimum blocking rate depending on threshold

problem: Severe inter-virtual-cell interference in ultra-dense networks with user-centric virtual cell paradigm due to irregular dense deployment.
method: Interference graph-based virtual cell merging with zero-forcing beamforming within clusters and multi-virtual-cell MMSE precoding for inter-cluster interference mitigation.
finding: The proposed approach achieves the average user rate of the overlapping-based grouping scheme with smaller virtual cell size and reduced signal processing complexity.
dataset: simulated UDN with uniformly distributed users and base stations
metric: average user rate, virtual cell size, complexity
baseline: grouping scheme based on virtual cell overlapping
result: comparable rate with smaller cell size and reduced complexity

problem: Channel state information acquisition overhead for virtual MIMO in multi-cell distributed antenna systems is overwhelming.
method: Coordinated transmissions using only large-scale CSIT with joint input covariance optimization via successive approximation and saddle-point theory.
finding: The proposed scheme converges quickly, yields significant performance gains over existing schemes, and achieves near-globally-optimal performance under diagonal input covariance constraint.
dataset: simulated multi-cell distributed antenna system
metric: ergodic sum rate
result: significant gains, near-globally-optimal with diagonal constraint

problem: Complex interference in ultra-dense networks and the need for load-aware virtual cell design to avoid cell overload.
method: Load-aware user-centric virtual cell scheme with optimal radius finding and adaptive joint communication activation threshold based on virtual cell load.
finding: The proposed scheme avoids cell overload and achieves a reasonable balance of resources and performance compared to no-load-aware case.
dataset: simulated 5G UDN downlink
metric: QoS, resource balance, cell load
result: avoids overload, balanced resources vs no-load-aware

problem: Complexity in automotive assembly systems with cooperating robots leads to long ramp-up times and delayed time-to-market.
method: Virtual commissioning (VC) techniques applied to an industrial robotic cell with cooperating robots, including complete virtual validation workflow.
finding: VC enables more efficient handling of assembly system complexity and significant reduction in ramp-up time.
dataset: industrial robotic assembly cell with cooperating robots
metric: ramp-up time, system validation efficiency

problem: Complex engineered microfluidic systems are needed for compartmentalized nucleic acid amplification, limiting accessibility.
method: Hydrogel-based virtual microfluidics with in-gel digital multiple displacement amplification (dMDA) applied to purified DNA, bacterial cells, and human microbiome samples.
finding: Virtual microfluidics enables whole-genome sequencing of single-cell MDA products with excellent coverage uniformity and markedly reduced chimerism compared to liquid MDA.
dataset: purified DNA, cultured bacterial cells, human microbiome samples
metric: coverage uniformity, chimerism rate
result: excellent coverage uniformity, reduced chimerism vs liquid MDA

problem: User-centric elastic resource management across multiple eNBs in TD-LTE systems.
method: SDN architecture for TD-LTE virtual cells.

problem: Integrative bioinformatics approaches toward building virtual cell models.
Whether reactive oxygen species (ROS) are relevant to apoptosis given observations of apoptosis occurring in virtual anaerobiosis.
method: Measurement of hydrogen peroxide production by cells and mitochondrial preparations under aerobic and virtually anaerobic conditions, with ceramide analog treatment.
finding: Cells and mitochondria produce similar levels of H2O2 under aerobic and anaerobic conditions; these levels are relevant to ceramide-induced apoptosis acting at complex III center o.
dataset: cells and mitochondrial preparations
metric: hydrogen peroxide levels
result: similar H2O2 levels in aerobic and anaerobic

problem: Whether intraepithelial T lymphocytes (IELs) of the small intestine can mature extrathymically or require thymic influence.
method: Review and discussion of evidence regarding IEL maturation and repertoire selection in the presence and absence of thymus.
finding: IEL precursors are present in the thymus and their differentiation pathway depends on thymic presence, challenging claims of fully thymus-independent intestinal T-cell development.
dataset: murine intraepithelial T lymphocytes

problem: Interference from co-locating virtual cells in ultra-low latency vehicular networks where open-loop transmissions prevent beamforming/interference alignment.
method: Multiuser detection (MUD) including reduced-computation ML-MUD and generalized linear MMSE equalizer with multi-antenna techniques.
finding: Modulation sensitivity of ML-MUD BER can be eased by multi-antenna; proposed reduced-computation ML-MUD and generalized LMMSE achieve satisfactory BER with lower complexity.
dataset: simulated virtual cell-based vehicular network
metric: bit error rate (BER), detection complexity
result: satisfactory BER with reduced complexity

problem: Extending user-centric virtual cells to V2X broadcast groups while maximizing served hotspots with minimum energy and guaranteed reliability.
method: Three-stage optimization: admission control, transmission weight selection, and power control for multi-user-centric virtual cells serving vehicle hotspots.
finding: The proposed solution increases network capacity, reliability, and power efficiency compared to baselines.
dataset: simulated 5G V2X network
metric: capacity, reliability, power efficiency
result: increased capacity, reliability, and power efficiency

problem: Scientific 3D data has been limited to 2D visualization, reducing understanding of complex cellular structures.
method: Virtual reality immersion using consumer VR headsets (Oculus Rift, HTC Vive) with 3D cell models built from serial block-face scanning electron microscope (SBEM) imaging data.
finding: VR immersion significantly improves students' understanding of cellular processes and enables new modes of scientific exploration and public engagement.
dataset: SBEM imaging data of cells
metric: student understanding (learning outcomes)
result: significant improvement in student understanding

problem: Rigorous estimation of forces on rolling leukocytes in blood is difficult due to the particulate, non-Newtonian nature of blood with red blood cells.
method: Lattice-Boltzmann simulation of leukocyte rolling in a virtual blood vessel with explicit red blood cell interactions at varying hematocrit.
finding: Red blood cells augment leukocyte binding via normal force and promote rolling via tangential force/torque; close envelopment by RBCs is necessary for significant force changes.
dataset: virtual blood vessel simulation (leukocyte + RBCs)
metric: normal force, tangential force, torque on leukocytes
result: RBC forces sufficient to increase leukocyte binding

problem: Load imbalance in downlink LTE-A systems requiring effective self-optimizing load balancing.
method: Virtual cell-breathing based load balancing (VCBLB) adjusting virtual transmission power to handover UEs from heavily to lightly loaded cells.
finding: VCBLB is feasible and more efficient than traditional load balancing schemes for LTE-A self-optimizing networks.
dataset: simulated LTE-A downlink
metric: load balance efficiency
result: more efficient than traditional schemes

problem: Overlapped user-centric virtual cells in UDN cause severe inter-VC interference; merging all overlapped VCs has high complexity overhead.
method: Joint overlapped VC merging based on overlapped TP ratio threshold and iterative beamforming via generalized WMMSE approach.
finding: The scheme balances system complexity and inter-VC interference and achieves significant throughput gain.
dataset: simulated ultra-dense network
metric: system sum rate, complexity overhead
result: significant system throughput gain with balanced complexity

problem: Worker assignment into virtual manufacturing cells under dual resource constraints in dynamic production environments.
method: Artificial neural networks (multilayer perceptron feed-forward) trained on simulated worker assignment datasets for virtual cellular manufacturing.
finding: The ANN-based approach effectively models complex worker assignment relationships and performs well under two cell configurations across different time periods.
dataset: simulated VCMS worker assignment data
metric: worker assignment accuracy

problem: Maximizing energy efficiency in user-centric virtual cell networks with beamforming and cluster formation under rate and power constraints.
method: WMMSE-based transformation with Lagrangian dual method yielding closed-form solutions, novel threshold-based AP clustering, and K-dimension grid search.
finding: The algorithm converges fast, is robust, and greatly outperforms benchmarks in energy efficiency and data rate.
dataset: simulated user-centric network with multi-antenna APs
metric: energy efficiency (EE), data rate
result: greatly outperforms benchmarks in EE and data rate

problem: Virtual resource allocation in small-cell networks with full-duplex self-backhauls is a mixed combinatorial non-convex optimization problem with high complexity.
method: Problem decomposition into two subproblems solved by ADMM-based distributed algorithm (convexified) and infrastructure provider-level convex optimization.
finding: The distributed scheme effectively handles virtualized resource allocation with reduced signaling overhead and scalability compared to centralized approaches.
dataset: simulated virtualized small-cell network with FD self-backhaul
metric: resource allocation efficiency, signaling overhead

problem: Multi-robot manufacturing cell design and commissioning requires flexibility that current physical prototyping approaches cannot efficiently provide.
method: Digital twin methodology with immersive VR interface for process design, virtual testbed validation, operator training, and real-time monitoring.
finding: The methodology enables validated assembly process automation design before physical implementation, applicable to operator training and feasibility studies.
dataset: assembly manufacturing use case with collaborative robots

problem: Synchronizing control between real and virtual industrial robots and enabling universal control through VR.
method: Digital twin synchronization model using Unity3D game engine for VR-based control and simulation of industrial robotic cells.
finding: The developed synchronization model enables real-time control of industrial robots through VR and can be modified for a wide range of industrial equipment.
dataset: industrial robotic cell (lab environment)

problem: Achieving low-latency V2V communication in highly dynamic vehicular networks underlaying V2I networks.
method: Time dynamic optimization with Lyapunov optimization theory to jointly optimize spectrum resources and constrain network switching rate, converted to deterministic problem.
finding: The proposed scheme approaches the best tradeoff between latency performance and network switching rate.
dataset: simulated V2V/V2I vehicular network
metric: latency, network switching rate
result: approaches best latency-switching tradeoff

problem: Precise reagent dispensing and cell culture on digital microfluidic devices is limited by active electrostatic methods.
method: Teflon-AF liftoff protocol for patterning hydrophilic sites on two-plate DMF devices to create virtual microwells for passive dispensing.
finding: Reproducible dispensing of 80–800 nL volumes with 0.7–13.8% CV; passive dispensing outperforms active electrostatic dispensing and is compatible with adherent cell culture.
dataset: Digital microfluidic device experiments
metric: CV (coefficient of variation), dispensed volume range
result: CV 0.7%–13.8% for 80–800 nL volumes

problem: Programming and safe monitoring of industrial robots requires dynamic updates to a virtual cell model.
dataset: Industrial robot system

problem: Frequent handovers and heavy signaling overhead degrade mobility management in ultra-dense networks, especially for fast-moving vehicles.
method: Proactive mobility management using virtual cell technique with LSTM-based trajectory prediction in an SDN controller.
finding: The proactive solution eliminates handover delay and reduces handover signaling cost by 35% compared to the reactive approach.
dataset: Real-world vehicle mobility dataset
metric: Handover signaling cost, handover delay, prediction accuracy
result: 35% signaling cost reduction

problem: Cell formation and production planning in dynamic environments where product mix and demand change over time, requiring reconfiguration.
method: Fuzzy goal programming for multi-objective optimization of virtual cellular manufacturing with worker flexibility over multi-period planning horizons.
finding: The model minimizes holding cost, backorder cost, and exceptional elements in a machine–part–worker incidence matrix under dynamic demand.
dataset: Illustrative manufacturing example
metric: Holding cost, backorder cost, exceptional elements

problem: Load imbalance among cells in LTE-A systems degrades system performance.
method: Load Prediction based Virtual Cell Breathing (LPVCB) scheme that uses load prediction and cell individual offset (CIO) control for load balancing.
finding: The proposed scheme improves system performance through more accurate load balancing using predictive virtual cell breathing.
dataset: LTE-A system simulation
metric: System performance (load balance)

problem: Directional channel modeling in microcells requires both deterministic and stochastic propagation simulation.
method: Virtual Cell Deployment Areas (VCDA) with cluster tracing: geometrical layouts define typical deployment areas, then multipath clusters are traced geometrically with stochastic small-scale fading.
finding: The approach was adopted by the COST259 DCM standard model for directional wireless channels.
dataset: Microcellular propagation scenarios
result: Adopted as COST259 DCM standard

problem: Determining the size of the active site in proteases, specifically papain.
dataset: Papain protease

problem: Analyzing the mechanical behavior of woven fibrous materials at the unit cell level.
method: Virtual tests at the unit cell level for mechanical behavior analysis.
dataset: Woven fibrous material

problem: Evaluating different lot sizing splitting strategies for virtual cells in distributed manufacturing layouts.
method: Computational simulation comparing three lot splitting strategies: no splitting, equally balanced splitting to K virtual cells, and unbalanced splitting using a makespan-based procedure.
finding: Separating parts to virtual cells significantly reduces makespan; the proposed unbalanced method performs better when total machines and part input orderings are considered.
dataset: Simulated distributed manufacturing layout
metric: Makespan
result: Significant makespan reduction

problem: Existing cell formation approaches neglect layout and handling strategies, failing to relate layout decisions to machine grouping and sharing.
method: A new cell formation approach integrating machine grouping and hybrid cellular layout design using overlapping GT cells, functional layout, and virtual manufacturing cells.
finding: Combining overlapping GT cells with functional layout reduces the need for machine duplication among cells, challenging traditional emphasis on independent cells.
dataset: Two illustrative manufacturing examples from literature
metric: Machine duplication reduction

problem: Wi-Fi Direct is not effective for spreading data over wide areas due to random partner selection and access control wait times.
method: Novel information diffusion method combining Wi-Fi Direct with DTN, using virtual cells based on device locations and a reaction–diffusion mathematical model for performance evaluation.
finding: The proposed virtual cell-based method enables efficient interference-free information diffusion; the reaction–diffusion model provides quantitative performance evaluation.
dataset: Simulated disaster area network
metric: Information diffusion performance

problem: 5G vehicular networks suffer from frequent handovers and connection drops due to ultra-dense small cells and high mobility.
method: Connectivity-oriented SARSA Reinforcement Learning (CO-SRL) algorithm for user-centric virtual cell management and handover reduction.
finding: CO-SRL reduced the number of handovers and cumulative handover time in realistic vehicular simulations.
dataset: Simulated 5G vehicular network
metric: Number of handovers, cumulative handover time
result: Reduced handovers and cumulative handover time

problem: Co-channel interference among sectors in relay enhanced cellular systems degrades performance.
method: Joint adaptive soft frequency reuse and virtual cell power control (JAP) algorithm for LTE-A relay enhanced cellular systems, using SIR-balance principle.
finding: The algorithm achieves frequency reuse factor of one among sectors and obtains both high average spectral efficiency and edge user throughput.
dataset: LTE-A relay enhanced cellular system simulation
metric: Average spectral efficiency, edge user throughput
result: High spectral efficiency and edge user throughput

problem: Optimizing spectral efficiency and energy efficiency in bidirectional distributed antenna systems with interference.
method: CCCP algorithm for non-convex power allocation optimization in bidirectional DAS with and without user-centric virtual cells, using D.C. programming and fractional programming.
finding: Bidirectional DAS with user-centric virtual cells achieves much better SE and EE than without virtual cells.
dataset: Simulated bidirectional DAS
metric: Spectral efficiency (SE), energy efficiency (EE)
baseline: Bidirectional DAS without user-centric virtual cells
result: SE and EE much better with virtual cells

problem: GAF algorithm's square grid-based equivalence does not match true radio diffusion, limiting energy conservation efficiency in wireless sensor networks.
method: Hexagonal virtual cell structure for GAF algorithm grouping, exploiting hexagonal geometry closer to radio diffusion patterns.
finding: The proposed algorithm wakes up only ~52% of nodes compared to GAF while maintaining network connectivity, with only 1.16% additional packet delay.
dataset: Wireless sensor network simulation
metric: Active node ratio, packet delay, network connectivity
result: 50.69% nodes awake vs GAF, +1.16% packet delay

problem: Spectral efficiency and energy efficiency optimization in distributed antenna systems with virtual cells.
metric: Spectral efficiency, energy efficiency

problem: Creating and managing user-centric virtual cells in ultra-dense 5G networks for high-mobility vehicle-to-network communications.
method: Vehicle-centric probabilistic approach using speed criteria and complex network metrics for virtual cell selection, processed in a centralized controller, simulated in ns3.
finding: The proposed approach enables more assertive virtual cell selection, improving IoV services through 5G networks.
dataset: ns3 simulation of 5G ultra-dense vehicular network
metric: Virtual cell selection accuracy
result: More assertive virtual cell selection

problem: Understanding the dynamic behavior of cells requires computational whole-cell models, which are becoming feasible with accumulating -omics data.
method: The E-CELL project constructs whole cell simulation models integrating genome, proteome, transcriptome, and metabolome data.
finding: Computer simulations of virtual cell models have revealed biological surprises; whole-cell simulation is identified as a grand challenge of the 21st century.
dataset: E-CELL virtual cell models

problem: Naive T cell responses erode with aging, but the mechanisms involving true naive vs virtual memory T cell precursors are not fully understood.
method: Analysis of TCR transgenic and wild-type old mice examining virtual memory (VM) CD8 T cell conversion, proliferation, and apoptosis in response to TCR and cytokine stimulation.
finding: Two separate age-related defects—reduction in true naive T cell precursors and impaired proliferative capacity of VM cells—combine to reduce naive T cell responses with aging; VM conversion depends on endogenous TCRα rearrangements.
dataset: TCR transgenic and wild-type old/young mice
metric: Proliferation rate, apoptosis rate, Ag-specific precursor frequency
result: ≥70% loss of Ag-specific CD8 T cell precursors in old mice

problem: Cell-free massive MIMO requires excessive backhaul overhead as all access points serve all users.
method: User-centric (UC) virtual cell approach where each user is served by a limited number of nearby access points in a cell-free massive MIMO system.
finding: The UC approach requires less backhaul overhead and outperforms the cell-free approach in achievable rate-per-user for the vast majority of users.
dataset: Simulated cell-free massive MIMO network
metric: Achievable rate-per-user, backhaul overhead
result: Outperforms CF in rate-per-user for majority of users

problem: User mobility causes frequent handovers and data rate degradation in 5G networks; rapid decisions are needed for virtual cell enable/disable.
method: RNN with Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) for predicting RSS values and triggering conditions for enabling/disabling CoMP-based virtual cell mode.
finding: The GRU-RNN model achieves 92% accuracy in predicting VC mode triggering conditions, enabling proactive virtual cell management.
dataset: Simulated 5G CoMP network with user mobility
metric: Prediction accuracy, data rate, handover count
result: 92% prediction accuracy

problem: Understanding the frequency behavior and mode competition in virtual-cathode oscillators.
method: Time-frequency analysis of microwave radiation from a virtual-cathode oscillator, with particle-in-cell simulations using MAGIC code.
finding: Clear evidence of mode hopping and frequency shifting was observed; dependence of mode competition on diode operation parameters was identified; simulations agreed with experiments.
dataset: Virtual-cathode oscillator experimental data
metric: Frequency behavior, mode competition
result: Simulation results in general agreement with experiments

problem: Reducing energy consumption in overlapping coverage areas of cellular networks.
method: Virtual cell clustering (VCC) technique that aggregates spare bandwidth from adjacent cells with a service partition optimization procedure.
finding: The proposed cooperative VCC scheme outperforms existing single-cell serving mode with significant energy saving.
dataset: Simulated cellular network with overlapping coverage
metric: Energy saving gain
result: Significant energy saving over existing mode

problem: Modeling feedbacks and oscillations in virtual cell systems.
dataset: Virtual cell VICE

problem: Optimizing power allocation and user association for highly mobile vehicular edge networks in highway scenarios.
method: Joint user association and power allocation using machine learning; a distributed SARL-MARL reinforcement learning algorithm for virtual cell formation.
finding: SARL-MARL obtains nearly genie-aided optimal solutions and outperforms existing schemes for vehicular edge networks.
dataset: Simulated highway vehicular edge network
metric: Weighted user sum rate, reliability
result: Nearly identical to genie-aided optimal

problem: Location privacy threats in ultra-dense 5G networks due to tracking attacks.
method: Analysis of the effect of mobile/access node densification and virtual cell aggregation on location privacy through simulation.
finding: Virtual cells can statistically reduce tracked mobile nodes by up to 10%; tracking attack success inversely relates to the number of moving nodes.
dataset: Simulated ultra-dense 5G network
metric: Percentage of tracked mobile nodes
result: Up to 10% reduction in tracked nodes

problem: Ultra-dense 5G networks face complex interference and high handover frequency; virtual cell design must balance data rates and resource overhead.
method: Fuzzy logic-based virtual cell design with stochastic geometry modeling and a fuzzy logic-based activation strategy selection (FASS) algorithm for threshold and resource allocation.
finding: The proposed solution effectively eliminates interference, improves system spectral efficiency, and dynamically adapts to different traffic loads and QoS requirements.
dataset: Simulated ultra-dense 5G network with imperfect CSI
metric: System spectral efficiency
result: Improved spectral efficiency with dynamic adaptation

problem: Existing Wi-Fi Direct methods randomly select communication partners, limiting information diffusion in disaster areas.
method: Novel information diffusion method using Wi-Fi Direct with virtual cells based on device location information, enabling interference-free scheduled communication.
finding: The scheduling control of the proposed method outperforms conventional methods in information diffusion effectiveness.
dataset: Simulated disaster area D2D network
metric: Information diffusion performance
result: Outperforms conventional methods

problem: Real-time control of dynamic clamp experiments requires fast, flexible simulation of nonlinear membrane conductances in living cells.
method: Real-Time Linux Dynamic Clamp system that simulates dozens of nonlinear conductances (deterministic and stochastic ion channels) at clock rates above 10 kHz.
finding: The system handles complex models including spatially extended excitable structures and multiple interacting cells in hard real-time, with freely available software.
dataset: Excitable cell models (nonlinear membrane mechanisms)
metric: Clock rate, real-time processing reliability
result: Clock rates well above 10 kHz

problem: Understanding virtual memory CD8 T cells as a memory-like but antigen-naïve population and their role in immune heterogeneity.
method: Review and synthesis of literature on TVM cell generation, maintenance, memory-like hallmarks, and signaling pathways during steady state, infection, and aging.
finding: TVM cells are semi-differentiated antigen-naïve CD8 T cells with memory-like hallmarks; a model integrating key signaling pathways during their generation is proposed.
dataset: Published studies on CD8 TVM cells

problem: Distributed radio resource management in infrastructure-less MANETs requires efficient channel allocation without centralized control.
method: Shared grid of virtual cells with pre-assigned communication channels using orthogonal frequency hopping patterns for clusters of 3 and 7 virtual cells.
finding: Average SINR is compared for random waypoint mobility model with two virtual cell designs; virtual cells enable legacy cellular reuse schemes in MANETs.
dataset: Simulated MANET with random waypoint mobility
metric: Average SINR

problem: Need for a general framework for simulating and visualizing cellular physiology.
dataset: Cellular physiology models

problem: Dynamic formation of virtual manufacturing cells requires accurate matching of component processing requirements to available machining capabilities.
method: Concurrent process and facility prototyping using generic 'resource elements' in a four-step methodology: requirement analysis, capability boundary definition, machine tool selection, and system evaluation.
finding: The methodology facilitates dynamic formation of virtual manufacturing structures by accurately assessing and matching processing requirements to existing facilities.
dataset: Machining shop component mix

problem: Screening and designing cell-penetrating peptides (CPPs) for drug delivery is time-consuming experimentally.
method: Computer-aided virtual screening using CellPPD, an in silico tool for prediction, screening, and designing of CPPs.
finding: In silico screening followed by experimental validation is a reliable, less time-consuming, and cost-effective approach for CPP development.
dataset: Cell-penetrating peptide datasets

problem: The original 2D virtual cell embedding (VCE) cartesian gridding method is inadequate for axisymmetric geometries.
method: Simple extension of VCE to handle axisymmetric cases by computing radial coordinates of cells and interfaces, tested on conical flow simulations.
finding: The extension achieves accuracy consistent with basic VCE surface representation in planar geometries and is useful for practical engineering design.
dataset: Conical flow simulations (Euler equations)
metric: Surface representation accuracy
result: Accuracy consistent with planar VCE

problem: Taxonomy and review of past research on virtual manufacturing cells to identify future research issues.
Virtual cell concept applied to mobile computer communications.
Simulation and experiment paradigm for virtual cell modeling in cell biology.
Cell biology modeling requires physically consistent, mathematically rigorous spatial simulation frameworks.
method: Virtual Cell framework with separated layers for biological models, physical mechanisms, geometry, mathematical models, and numerical methods, with remote database and cluster computation.
finding: The separation of layers reduces software complexity and clarifies modeling decisions; the framework provides scalable spatial modeling and simulation for cell biology.
dataset: Cell biology models

problem: Biology education needs immersive virtual environments for teaching cellular biology and scientific reasoning.
method: JavaMOO-based Virtual Cell desktop immersive virtual environment with deployable server architectures, robust simulations, and high-quality animations.
finding: The Virtual Cell platform addresses technical challenges in biology education including server architecture, simulation robustness, and interactive animations.
dataset: Biology education (cellular biology curriculum)

problem: Scheduling of virtual manufacturing cells (VMCs) is a complex optimization problem.
method: Genetic algorithm based heuristic for VMC scheduling.

problem: Analysis of single molecule imaging experiments is complicated by stochastic events, instrument noise, and limited per-molecule information; cross-checking with simulation is needed.
method: Object-oriented 3D virtual cell model simulating single molecule dynamics (movements, binding events) with realistic fluorescence imaging output including noise and emission fluctuations.
finding: The model accurately reproduces reported single molecule experiments and can predict outcomes of future experiments with realistic simulated fluorescence video data.
dataset: Single molecule imaging experiments
metric: Reproduction accuracy of reported experiments
result: Accurately reproduces reported experiments

problem: Analyzing downlink ergodic rate in virtual cell-based Cloud Radio Access Networks (CRAN) with cooperative RRH transmission.
method: Stochastic geometry model with Poisson point processes for RRH and MS locations; derived tractable integral expression for downlink ergodic rate of (N,K) virtual cell-based CRAN.
finding: In interference-limited regime, ergodic rate depends only on MS-to-RRH intensity ratio; cooperation among multiple RRHs significantly improves downlink ergodic rate.
dataset: Simulated CRAN with Poisson-distributed RRHs and MSs
metric: Downlink ergodic rate
baseline: Conventional cellular networks (single RRH per cell)
result: Significant ergodic rate improvement with RRH cooperation

problem: Determining under what conditions virtual cell manufacturing layouts are advantageous compared to job shop and traditional cellular layouts.
method: Simulation and design of experiments comparing virtual cell, job shop, and cellular layouts with identical capacities under the same demand, using priority-based production sequencing.
finding: Virtual cell layout performance depends on specific conditions; a significant number of factors and levels were tested to delimit the scope where virtual cells lead to competitive advantages.
dataset: Simulated manufacturing systems with identical capacities
metric: Performance comparison metrics (not specified)

problem: Resource allocation in cellular networks needs an approach between fully centralized and fully decentralized optimization.
method: Fog/neighborhood-based optimization using virtual cells of multiple base stations, with hierarchical clustering for cell formation and several methods for NP-hard joint channel access and power allocation.
finding: Proper design of fog optimization results in little degradation relative to centralized optimization even with many virtual cells; improper design leads to significant sum rate decrease.
dataset: Simulated cellular network
metric: System sum rate
result: Little degradation vs centralized with proper design

problem: Sharing RAN infrastructure among multiple virtual mobile operators (tenants) to reduce CAPEX/OPEX in the face of growing mobile data demand.
method: Designed a RAN Multi-tenant cell Slicing Controller (RMSC) with three architectures: fully distributed, semi-centralized, and fully centralized.
finding: Proposed RMSC schemes significantly outperform traditional static slicing and centralized load balancing solutions for multi-tenant mobile networks under uneven user distributions.
dataset: Simulated multi-tenant mobile network scenarios with uneven user location distributions
metric: Resource allocation performance vs. baseline solutions
baseline: Distributed static slicing, centralized load balancing
result: Significant outperformance of traditional solutions

problem: Estimating charge separation and recombination rate constants of a bipolar photogalvanic cell through simulation.
method: Developed a Virtual Basic-based virtual device simulator for bipolar photogalvanic cells; fabricated an actual device using [Ru(bpy)3²⁺] sensitizer and Prussian Blue mediator.
finding: Charge separation and recombination rate constants were estimated at 5×10² and 6×10⁹ mol⁻¹cm³s⁻¹ respectively; photocurrent response enables estimation of kd and kr.
dataset: Simulated and fabricated bipolar photogalvanic cell with [Ru(bpy)3²⁺]/Prussian Blue
metric: Short-circuit photocurrent (JSC), open-circuit photovoltage (VOC), fill-factor
result: JSC=2.3μA/cm², VOC=0.118V, fill-factor=20.5%

problem: Reducing power consumption of base stations in wireless networks, which is a significant OPEX concern in the ITC sector.
method: Implemented a virtual cell concept on base stations and proposed an algorithm for low traffic conditions to improve energy efficiency.
finding: The virtual cell concept applied to base stations can reduce power consumption under low traffic conditions.
dataset: Simulated wireless network base stations
metric: Power consumption

problem: Understanding how virtual memory T cells contribute to effector and resident memory responses within infected tissues.
method: Investigated CCR2⁺ and CCR2⁻ virtual memory T cell subsets and their differential roles in immune responses.
finding: CCR2⁺ and CCR2⁻ virtual memory T cells differentially contribute to effector and resident memory responses within infected tissues.

problem: Programs often exceed available physical memory, requiring overlay techniques that burden programmers; automating memory management through virtual memory is needed.
method: Studied replacement algorithms for virtual-storage computer systems that automatically manage memory-overlay functions.
finding: Virtual memory with automatic replacement algorithms can free programmers from manual overlay management by providing a sufficiently large address range.
dataset: Virtual-storage computer system simulations

problem: Efficient allocation of wireless resources to multiple service providers in virtualized multi-cell LTE networks while respecting SLA constraints.
method: Developed a centralized heuristic for radio resource block allocation in multi-cell LTE; proposed an iterative DC programming solution for non-convex power allocation.
finding: The heuristic produces results close to the iterative method; SLA contracts cause significant rate reduction; the heuristic meets required rates over multiple scheduling periods even if it cannot in a single period.
dataset: Simulated multi-cell LTE network with multiple service providers
metric: Network-wide sum rate, SLA satisfaction
result: Heuristic close to iterative method results

problem: Obtaining a comprehensive brain-wide, cellular-level connectivity map (mesoscale connectome) for the mouse, which is fundamental for understanding neural information processing.
method: Used EGFP-expressing adeno-associated viral vectors for axonal tracing and high-throughput serial two-photon tomography for imaging, registered into a common 3D reference space.
finding: Created the Allen Mouse Brain Connectivity Atlas revealing connectional strength distribution, network symmetry, 3D topography via virtual tractography, and segregation/integration of cortico-thalamic pathways.
dataset: Mouse brain, Allen Mouse Brain Connectivity Atlas
metric: Connectional strength, network symmetry properties
baseline: C. elegans microscale connectome (only prior complete dataset)
result: Whole-brain connectivity matrix in common 3D reference space

problem: Optimizing performance of dual virtual cell-based systems in cognitive overlay convergent heterogeneous networks with increasing data traffic.
method: Proposed a Distributed Wireless Communication System with Active and Candidate Virtual Cells using Space-Time Trellis Codes for multi-user high-rate data transmission.
finding: The dual virtual cell system with cognitive ability and STTC can protect link quality and enable high-rate data transmission; quantitative simulation results confirm system effectiveness under varying parameters.
dataset: Simulated overlay convergent network with heterogeneous base stations
metric: System performance under varying environmental parameters

problem: Identifying novel GSK-3β kinase inhibitors as potential Alzheimer's disease therapeutics that reduce tau aggregation.
method: Virtual screening of >1.1M compounds (ZINC and in-house databases) using GOLD docking, followed by luminescent kinase assay and cell assays in HEK293 and SH-SY5Y cells.
finding: Identified VB-003 (IC50=0.25μM) and VB-008/AM404 (IC50=5.4μM) as GSK-3β inhibitors reducing tau aggregation; VB-008 also enhanced HSPB1/GRP78 expression and promoted neurite outgrowth.
dataset: >1.1M compounds (ZINC + in-house), HEK293 cells, ΔK280 tauRD-DsRed SH-SY5Y cells
metric: IC50, tau aggregation reduction, phospho-GSK-3β expression

problem: Conceptualizing virtual manufacturing cells for flexible production systems.
Understanding the protective and pathological roles of virtual memory T (TVM) cells in immune-related diseases, which remain largely unknown.
method: Review of current literature on TVM cell characteristics, immunological properties, and roles in infections and cancers.
finding: TVM cells play first-line defense roles similar to innate cells against bacterial and viral infections, with emerging evidence for roles in various immune-related diseases including cancers.

problem: Reducing CSI overhead and inter-virtual-cell interference in ultra dense networks where overlapping virtual cells form clusters.
method: Proposed hierarchical beamforming: intra-cluster Zero-Forcing block diagonalization with perfect CSI, inter-cluster iterative power allocation via generalized weighted MMSE using only large-scale CSI.
finding: The proposed hierarchical scheme achieves significant performance gain over traditional schemes while requiring less channel state information.
dataset: Simulated ultra dense network with virtual cell clusters
metric: Achievable rate, CSI overhead
result: Significant performance gain with less CSI

problem: Determining whether rodent hippocampal place coding has a homologue in humans or whether human navigation relies on a different, visually-based mechanism.
method: Directly recorded from 317 neurons in human medial temporal and frontal lobes while subjects explored and navigated a virtual town.
finding: Found place-responsive cells primarily in hippocampus and landmark view-responsive cells in parahippocampal region; cells throughout frontal and temporal lobes responded to navigational goals and conjunctions of place, goal, and view.
dataset: 317 neurons recorded from human medial temporal and frontal lobes during virtual navigation
metric: Neuronal firing rate at specific spatial locations and landmark views
result: Evidence for human spatial navigation neural code with place cells and view cells

problem: Achieving practical multiple access in ultra-low latency virtual-cell-based vehicular networks without CSI feedback.
method: Applied multiuser detection (MUD) with sphere decoding for downlink interference suppression, exploiting downlink transmission characteristics for complexity reduction.
finding: Low-complexity MUD with sphere decoding achieves a favorable tradeoff between BER performance and detection complexity, enabling further reliability and lower latency in the downlink.
dataset: Simulated virtual-cell vehicular network downlink
metric: Bit error rate (BER), detection complexity, latency
baseline: Beamforming/interference alignment (unsuitable due to CSI feedback requirement)
result: Improved reliability and lower latency via sphere decoding

problem: Extracting quantitative parameters from live cell fluorescence imaging data using computational modeling.
method: Used the Virtual Cell simulation environment for analysis of fluorescence imaging data.
dataset: Live cell fluorescence imaging data

problem: Joint resource allocation combining dynamic user grouping, multi-cell cooperation, and resource block allocation for uplink virtual MIMO systems.
method: Developed dynamic multi-cell user grouping with MMSE equalization and adaptive modulation under BER constraint; proposed an efficient iterative Hungarian algorithm based on user/resource partitions.
finding: The proposed joint algorithm achieves better system throughput with BER guarantee than fixed user grouping and other schemes, with significantly reduced computational complexity.
dataset: Simulated multi-cell SC-FDMA uplink virtual MIMO system
metric: System throughput, BER, computational complexity
baseline: Fixed user grouping algorithm, other proposed schemes in literature
result: Better throughput with BER guarantee, close-to-optimal with lower complexity

problem: Developing virtual reality software tools for simulation of robotics and manufacturing cells.
Detecting and resolving resource conflicts in virtual manufacturing cells when new tasks are dynamically inserted into an initial schedule.
method: Used Bayesian network for resource conflict detection and critical chain method for conflict resolution based on virtual cell and process priority.
finding: The proposed Bayesian network detection and critical chain resolution methods are feasible and effective for handling resource conflicts from new task insertions in virtual cells.
dataset: Case study of virtual manufacturing cell scheduling
metric: Feasibility and effectiveness of conflict detection/resolution
result: Verified feasibility and effectiveness through case study

problem: Whether chemotherapy-treated cancer cells can activate CD8 T cells independent of tumor-derived MHC class I, and whether this can be exploited for immunotherapy of MHC I-deficient cancers.
method: In vitro and in vivo experiments with chemotherapy-treated cancer cells, flow cytometry for VM phenotyping, PI3K pathway analysis, humanized mouse models, and primary human CD8 T cells.
finding: Chemotherapy-treated cancer cells directly activate CD8 T cells in an MHC class I-independent manner with virtual memory phenotypes; activation requires cell-cell contact and PI3K pathway; VM CD8 T cells contribute to superior therapeutic effects on MHC I-deficient tumors.
dataset: Mouse tumor models, humanized mouse models, primary human CD8 T cells, human lymphomas
metric: VM CD8 T cell frequency, tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte analysis
result: MHC I-independent CD8 T cell activation with VM phenotype; superior effect on MHC I-deficient tumors

problem: Understanding the complex dynamical behaviors of intracellular cardiac Ca²⁺ handling, including sparks, oscillations, and traveling waves.
method: Combined experimental approaches with virtual cell computational simulation to integrate and analyze subcellular Ca²⁺ dynamics information.
finding: Intracellular Ca²⁺ handling involves coordinated interactions of numerous cellular structures producing coherent spatiotemporal patterns; virtual cell tools are essential for integrating experimental data with simulations.
dataset: Cardiac cell intracellular Ca²⁺ dynamics

problem: Training future professionals in virtual commissioning (VC) technologies for industrial robotic cells to shorten lead times and improve product quality.
method: Educational case study comparing conventional robotic cell control development against digital twin-based virtual commissioning approach.
finding: Virtual commissioning with a digital twin offers clear benefits over conventional approaches for developing and validating robotic cell control systems in educational settings.
dataset: Educational robotic cell case study
metric: Lead time, product quality (qualitative comparison)
result: VC with digital twin demonstrated benefits over conventional approach

problem: Understanding the dynamic topology of the mitochondrial inner membrane and its implications for bioenergetic function.
method: Electron tomography for structural imaging; computer modeling using the Virtual Cell program to simulate diffusion and ATP output within cristae compartments.
finding: Inner membrane is pleomorphic with narrow tubular junctions connecting cristae; transitions between morphological states involve membrane fusion/fission; Virtual Cell simulations show narrow cristae junctions restrict ADP diffusion and decrease ATP output inside cristae.
dataset: Mitochondria from various cell types including yeast
metric: ADP diffusion rates, ATP output
baseline: Textbook baffle-like fold model of inner membrane
result: Narrow cristae junctions cause ADP depletion and decreased ATP output inside cristae

problem: Moving toward computational (in silico) modeling of cellular processes as a complement to in vivo and in vitro approaches.
Achieving fault tolerant control of a proton exchange membrane fuel cell system.
method: Takagi-Sugeno virtual actuators for fault tolerant control.
dataset: Proton exchange membrane fuel cell

problem: Traditional lecture-based teaching of 3D molecular concepts in virology, cell culture, and molecular biology is limited; immersive approaches are needed to support growing biotechnology workforce demands.
method: Review of virtual reality (VR) applications for immersive, active, and performative learning in molecular life sciences, accelerated by Covid-19 pandemic shifts in education delivery.
finding: Immersive VR offers new entry points for learning abstract molecular concepts and remains relevant for face-to-face teaching; engagement of disciplinary and technical experts is key for implementation.

problem: Evaluating the predictive value of neutrophil leucocytosis for diagnosing bacterial throat infection when infectious mononucleosis prevalence is high.
method: Clinical epidemiological analysis of white cell count utility and reflection on historical antibiotic prescribing patterns.
finding: The positive predictive value of neutrophil leucocytosis for bacterial throat infection is low when infectious mononucleosis prevalence is high; historical wholesale antibiotic prescribing may have contributed to the decline of post-streptococcal diseases.
dataset: Young patient populations with sore throat
metric: Positive predictive value
result: Low predictive value when mononucleosis prevalence is high

problem: Integrating Period Batch Control (PBC) production planning with virtual manufacturing cells whose configurations change periodically.
method: Developed a model aligning PBC principles with virtual cell design goals; tested with scheduling software simulating different scheduling rules over four production weeks.
finding: PBC parameter choices (period length, stage number/contents) directly impact virtual cell configurations, machine sharing, and utilization; the model successfully integrates PBC with dynamic virtual cells.
dataset: Case study of furniture production, 4 production weeks
metric: Machine sharing, machine utilization
baseline: Classical group technology cells with PBC
result: Demonstrated PBC parameter impact on virtual cell configurations

problem: Computationally screening the vast space of organic semiconductor compounds for solar cells and light emitting diodes, balancing efficiency and accuracy.
method: Virtual screening using computational methods at varying levels of accuracy and efficiency; discussed chemical design of non-fullerene acceptors, TADF emitters, and nanographenes.
finding: Effective virtual screening for organic semiconductors rests on a delicate balance between computational efficiency and accuracy; the approach can point to new, better materials not yet synthesized.
dataset: Organic semiconductor compound libraries (non-fullerene acceptors, TADF emitters, nanographenes)
metric: Computational efficiency vs. accuracy tradeoff

problem: Finite element method encounters dispersion errors in mid-frequency acoustic problems due to its overly stiff nature.
method: Proposed a cell-based smoothed radial point interpolation method (CS-RPIM) with virtual nodes using generalized gradient smoothing for solving the Helmholtz equation.
finding: CS-RPIM with virtual nodes provides close-to-exact stiffness, leading to conspicuous decrease in dispersion errors; demonstrated high computational efficiency and insensitivity to mesh distortion.
dataset: Numerical acoustic benchmark problems (Helmholtz equation)
metric: Dispersion error, accuracy, computational efficiency, mesh distortion sensitivity
baseline: FEM-T4 (four-node tetrahedral), MIR-H8 (eight-node hexahedral with modified integration)
result: Significant improvement over FEM-T4 and MIR-H8 in dispersion error and accuracy

problem: Creating virtual groups that combine lean and agile manufacturing characteristics through enhanced production flow analysis.
Whether virtual memory T (TVM) cells can act as pathogenic effectors of inflammatory disease, specifically alopecia areata.
method: Identified a TVM cell-originated CD44super-high CD49dlo CD8+ T cell subset; investigated induction by IL-12/IL-15/IL-18 stimulation and NKG2D-dependent cytotoxicity mechanism.
finding: A TVM-originated CD44s-hi CD49dlo CD8+ T cell subset with tissue-residency features causes alopecia areata via NKG2D-dependent innate-like cytotoxicity augmented by IL-15.
dataset: Mouse models of alopecia areata
metric: NKG2D-dependent cytotoxicity, disease onset
result: TVM-originated subset identified as pathogenic driver of alopecia areata

problem: Limited frequency resources for unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) cause communication failures due to interference when numerous UAs operate at high density.
method: Proposed a multilayer virtual-cell-based TDMA resource allocation scheme that increases simultaneous UA communications by considering UA-to-ground-station distance.
finding: The multilayer virtual cell scheme increases the number of simultaneously communicating UAs and improves throughput compared to existing schemes.
dataset: Simulated unmanned aircraft system network
metric: Number of simultaneously communicating UAs, throughput
result: More efficient than existing schemes in simultaneous UA communication

problem: Tissue exhaustion and section-level mismatch limit immunohistochemistry (IHC) on cytology cell block specimens; multiplex IHC is costly.
method: Digitized cell block IHC slides with whole-slide scanner, aligned via image registration using ImageJ with custom code, recolored layers to create virtual multiplex IHC images.
finding: Virtual multiplex IHC successfully demonstrated coexpression of multiple antibodies (membranous, cytoplasmic, nuclear) without additional tissue consumption or multiplex IHC cost; mean registration accuracy was 8.40±5.52μm.
dataset: 20 cell block preparations (19 carcinomas, 1 melanoma)
metric: Mean distance between cell clusters after registration
result: Registration accuracy 8.40±5.52μm, 20/20 cases successful

problem: Defining and calculating molecular distribution functions from pair potential energies to describe thermodynamic properties of gases and liquids.
method: Derived distribution functions as power series in fugacity or inverse volume for gases; for condensed systems derived integral equations; introduced virtual cell population probabilities connecting to the cell approximation method.
finding: Established a direct connection between condensing-system theory and cell approximations for liquids; the virtual cell method permits consistent higher-order approximations for evaluating thermodynamic functions of liquids.
dataset: Theoretical molecular systems (gases and liquids)
result: Unified framework connecting condensing-system theory and cell approximations

problem: Multi-objective optimization for virtual cells as a service in cloud environments.
Predicting liquid water intrusion and transport properties in fuel cell gas diffusion layers (GDLs).
method: Developed a cluster-based full morphology (CFM) model to simulate water intrusion into dry GDL microstructure from μ-CT; compared with experimental μ-CT reconstructions at varying saturation levels.
finding: CFM simulations showed 56–95.7% agreement with μ-CT for water voxels (29–92.3% saturation); effective diffusivity predictions had max 20% error below 40% saturation; through-plane diffusivity was underpredicted by 2x.
dataset: GDL microstructure from μ-CT imaging
metric: Water voxel agreement (%), effective diffusivity error
baseline: μ-CT experimental reconstructions, literature effective diffusivity values
result: 56–95.7% voxel agreement, max 20% diffusivity error below 40% saturation

problem: Forming intelligent virtual manufacturing cells in cloud-based design and manufacturing environments.
Understanding the dynamics of topological defects (spiral wave patterns) on biological cell membranes and their parallels to quantum systems.
method: Combined direct experimental observations of starfish egg cell surfaces with mathematical continuum modeling and chemical perturbations to analyze spiral wave defect dynamics.
finding: Spiral wave defects on starfish egg cells exhibit complex braiding, pair creation, and annihilation dynamics consistent with generic continuum theory predictions, suggesting parallels between information transport in living and quantum systems.
dataset: Starfish egg cell membrane spiral wave patterns
metric: Defect braiding dynamics, pair creation/annihilation rates
result: Experimental dynamics agree with continuum theory predictions

problem: Comparing chromosomal stability of neural cells derived from different reprogramming methods (pluripotent stem cells, adult stem cells, transdifferentiation).
method: Virtual karyotyping (e-karyotyping) of over 500 neural cell samples from human and mouse sources.
finding: Neural cells from pluripotent or adult stem cells show notable genomic instability, while transdifferentiated cells are more chromosomally stable unless reprogrammed using pluripotency factors.
dataset: >500 human and mouse neural cell samples
metric: Chromosomal stability via e-karyotyping
baseline: Pluripotent stem cell-derived and adult stem cell-derived neural cells
result: Transdifferentiated cells more chromosomally stable than stem cell-derived

problem: Characterizing transmembrane potential (Vm) responses in cardiac cell strands of different curvature during uniform electric-field stimulation.
method: Optical mapping with voltage-sensitive dye on linear and U-shaped cardiac strands (100–150μm width); computational modeling of passive and active membrane current components.
finding: Curved strands exhibit field responses separable into rapid passive, slow hyperpolarizing, and delayed depolarizing components with characteristic amplitude, spatial, and temporal signatures attributable to passive behavior and active membrane currents.
dataset: Linear and U-shaped cardiac cell strands (100-150μm width)
metric: Transmembrane potential (Vm) components: amplitude, spatial and temporal signatures
result: Identified separable Vm components in curved strands with distinct signatures

problem: Clarifying whether lymphopenia-induced memory CD8 T cells and virtual memory CD8 T cells are distinct subsets or the same population.
method: Comparative review of cytokine requirements, TCR specificity roles, gene expression signatures, and immune responses of both T cell types.
finding: Lymphopenia-induced memory T cells and virtual memory T cells most likely represent a single subset, proposed to be called 'homeostatic memory T cells'.
result: Proposed unification as single subset: homeostatic memory T cells

problem: User clustering and multi-dimensional resource allocation in user-centric overlapped virtual cells of ultra-dense 5G networks to mitigate interference and improve spectrum efficiency.
method: Proposed graph-based user clustering with novel edge weight design incorporating space and power cooperation gains; used Greedy Tree Growing Algorithm (GTGA) with Gaussian-distributed threshold for dynamic cluster sizing.
finding: The proposed approach effectively reduces complexity, mitigates interference, and improves spectrum efficiency in ultra-dense networks.
dataset: Simulated ultra-dense 5G network with overlapped virtual cells
metric: Spectrum efficiency (SE), computational complexity
result: Effective interference mitigation and SE improvement

problem: Extending beyond 5G toward 6G with flexible, on-demand coverage using virtual mobile small cells to overcome fixed deployment limitations.
method: Proposed virtual mobile small cells (MSCs) created on demand using NFV and SDN, forming collaborative wireless networks with lightweight security and energy-efficient RF.
finding: MSC architecture enables reliable and efficient cell offloading with reduced protocol signaling latency, providing localized, ubiquitous, and reliable connectivity toward 6G.
dataset: Simulated mobile small cell network
metric: Cell offloading efficiency, protocol signaling latency
result: Demonstrated benefits for reliable and efficient cell offloading

problem: Sensing MMP9 activity of single suspended and adhered cancer cells.
method: Developed a virtual-droplet system for single-cell MMP9 activity detection.
dataset: Single suspended and adhered cancer cells
metric: MMP9 activity

problem: The main obstacle to broader virtual commissioning adoption is the high modeling time and effort required to create proper simulation models, which is disconnected from the project development lifecycle.
method: Proposed a semi-automatic method to transform 3D geometry of a production cell into a simulation-enabled virtual representation using VDI 2860-based behavior modeling and AutomationML, embedded in a VC workflow.
finding: The semi-automatic transformation method successfully creates high-fidelity simulation models from 3D geometry combined with intuitive behavior descriptions; demonstrated on a conveyor production cell using multiple commercial tools.
dataset: Production cell with conveyors (case study)
metric: Modeling time and effort, simulation fidelity
result: Semi-automatic method demonstrated on conveyor production cell

problem: Identification of small-molecule inhibitors targeting the urokinase receptor (uPAR) to block breast tumor metastasis.
method: Virtual screening of uPAR, followed by synthesis, biochemical assays, cell-based invasion/migration/adhesion assays, angiogenesis assays, pharmacokinetic characterization, and in vivo mouse metastasis model.
finding: Compound IPR-69 showed leadlike properties, oral bioavailability (peak ~40 μM, t1/2 ~2h), 20% tumor volume reduction, and reduced metastasis in NOD-SCID mice.
dataset: MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells, NOD-SCID mice with TMD-231 mammary fat pad inoculation
metric: IC50 (invasion, migration, adhesion, angiogenesis, cell growth)
result: IC50 ~30 μM (invasion/migration), IC50 3 μM (angiogenesis), IC50 6-18 μM (growth), 20% tumor volume reduction in vivo

problem: Scheduling operations in virtual manufacturing cells with outsourcing and sequence-dependent setup times to minimize makespan and transportation cost.
method: Bi-objective mixed integer programming solved by ε-constraint method (exact) and a bi-objective genetic algorithm (GA) for large-scale NP-hard instances.
finding: The proposed model outperforms base models, and the GA obtains efficient solutions in much less computational time compared to the ε-constraint method.
dataset: Numerical examples of virtual manufacturing cell scheduling problems
metric: makespan, total transportation cost, computational time
baseline: ε-constraint exact method, base scheduling models
result: GA achieves comparable solution quality in much less computational time

problem: Reducing energy consumption in ultradense small cell networks by maximizing the number of small cells in sleeping state via wireless virtualization.
method: Wireless resource mapping algorithm based on virtual cell division and alternate updating of small cell mapping and resource allocation.
finding: The algorithm effectively reduces system energy consumption and required wireless resource amount while satisfying users' QoS constraints.
dataset: Simulated ultradense small cell network
metric: energy consumption, wireless resource utilization, convergence
result: Effective reduction in system energy consumption while maintaining QoS

problem: Minimizing overall transmit power in a virtual-cell downlink with distributed antennas while satisfying QoS constraints.
method: Sequential power allocation for single-user case; successive convex approximation (single condensation method) for multi-user case, accounting for both channel estimation and data transmission power.
