finding: The proposed power allocation strategies achieve up to 45% power savings compared to equal power allocation baseline.
dataset: Simulated distributed wireless communication system
metric: total transmit power savings
result: up to 45% power savings

problem: Understanding how elastic coupling between cells in the inner ear enhances hearing across vertebrate species.
method: Yoking real and virtual cells to test cochlear amplification theory.
finding: Elastic coupling between cells in the inner ear enhances the hearing of amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals, confirming cochlear amplification theory.
dataset: Inner ear cells of amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals

problem: Describing how molecular components interact to create complex cellular behavior underlying development and disease, after cataloguing individual components.
method: Review of bioinformatics and genomics approaches for modeling the virtual cell, including systems-level interaction mapping.
finding: The next challenge after component cataloguing is describing component interactions to understand complex cellular behavior in development and disease.

problem: Batch splitting in scheduling of virtual manufacturing cells with capacity-constrained bottleneck machines.
method: Bi-level multi-objective mathematical model with separate decision levels for bottleneck and non-bottleneck machines.
finding: The bi-level model provides scheduling results and batch splitting strategies that maximize bottleneck machine utilization while improving additional objectives at the second level.
dataset: Application example of virtual manufacturing cell scheduling
metric: bottleneck machine utilization, scheduling objectives

problem: Providing deep proteomic profiling of mpkCCD collecting duct cells as a data resource and characterizing aberrant protein mobility on SDS-PAGE.
method: Three levels of fractionation (differential centrifugation, SDS-PAGE, HPLC) followed by tandem mass spectrometry; virtual Western blots generated from gel slice mapping.
finding: Identified 6,766 proteins over eight orders of magnitude abundance; >15% run aberrantly on SDS-PAGE due to various mechanisms; discovered a previously unrecognized aquaporin-2 isoform (<20 kDa).
dataset: mpkCCD mouse collecting duct cell line, 34.6 GB spectral data
metric: number of proteins identified, apparent vs calculated molecular weight agreement
result: 6,766 proteins identified, >15% aberrant mobility

problem: Minimizing end-to-end power consumption of cell-free massive MIMO deployed on virtualized cloud radio access networks (V-CRAN).
method: Joint optimization of radio, optical fronthaul, and virtualized cloud processing resources via mixed binary second-order cone programming solved by branch-and-bound.
finding: Cell-free massive MIMO substantially increases maximum rate with almost the same energy per bit, and is more power-efficient than conventional small-cell V-CRAN above 1 bit/s/Hz spectral efficiency.
dataset: Simulated V-CRAN with cell-free massive MIMO
metric: end-to-end power consumption, spectral efficiency, energy per bit
result: more power-efficient above 1 bit/s/Hz spectral efficiency

problem: Security analysis of mobile edge computing in virtualized small cell networks.
Discovering potent and selective CYP1A1 enzyme inhibitors for cancer chemoprevention via virtual screening.
method: Combined ligand-based e-pharmacophore screening and structure-based virtual screening (docking, MMGB/SA binding affinity) of 50,000 compounds, validated in vitro in Sacchrosomes and live human cells, with molecular dynamics simulations.
finding: Compound 5121780 potently and selectively inhibits CYP1 enzymes (IC50 269/30/56 nM for CYP1A1/1A2/1B1) with no detectable CYP2/CYP3 inhibition at 10 μM, rescued cells from B[a]P toxicity and overcame cisplatin resistance.
dataset: 50,000 compound library, CYP1A1-Sacchrosomes, CYP-expressing HEK293 cells
metric: % inhibition at 10 μM, IC50
result: IC50 = 269 nM (CYP1A1), 30 nM (CYP1A2), 56 nM (CYP1B1); 97% inhibition at 10 μM; 32/300 hits >50% inhibition

problem: Comparing software packages for kinetic modeling of biochemical networks to guide tool selection.
method: Comparison of 12 software packages on functionality, reliability, efficiency, user-friendliness, compatibility, SBML support, and multicompartmentalization.
finding: GEPASI is best for non-expert users with simple models; Jarnac/JDesigner preferred for large-scale models; Virtual Cell is the most versatile with clearest multicompartmentalization support. SBML compatibility is problematic in many packages.
dataset: 12 kinetic modeling software packages
metric: functionality, reliability, efficiency, user-friendliness, SBML compatibility
result: Virtual Cell most versatile; GEPASI best for non-experts

problem: Scheduling of virtual manufacturing cells using mixed integer programming.
Identifying novel nonpeptidic inhibitors of human thymidylate synthase (hTS) for ovarian cancer treatment.
method: Virtual screening targeting hTS, followed by enzymatic inhibition assays, X-ray crystallography, and cancer cell line activity testing.
finding: Compound 5 is the first nonpeptidic inhibitor binding the inactive hTS conformation, with Ki of 1.3 μM and anticancer activity against ovarian cancer cells matching cisplatin potency.
dataset: Human thymidylate synthase, ovarian cancer cell lines
metric: Ki, anticancer potency comparison
result: Ki = 1.3 μM, same potency as cisplatin against ovarian cancer cells

problem: Obtaining better representation of human proteome information by combining virtual and experimental 2DE approaches.
method: Experimental 2DE coupled with ESI LC-MS/MS and MALDI-TOF MS protein identification; comparison of experimental vs theoretical pI/Mw parameters.
finding: Major proteoforms in HepG2 cells have pI/Mw close to theoretical values, while minor protein species are heavily modified; modifications are even more pronounced in plasma proteome.
dataset: HEPG2 cell line, human blood plasma
metric: correlation between theoretical and experimental pI/Mw
baseline: theoretical pI/Mw from Compute pI/Mw tool
result: major proteoforms match theoretical; minor species heavily modified

problem: Identifying key challenges for broadcasting virtual reality content to large audiences using 5G millimeter wave small cell networks.
method: Performance evaluation using realistic 5G network settings to assess technology potential and practical constraints for VR broadcast.
finding: VR broadcast can be supported under ideal conditions using 5G mmWave small cells with slightly higher system bandwidth requirements.
dataset: Simulated 5G millimeter wave small cell network
metric: data rate, system bandwidth, latency, reliability
result: VR broadcast feasible with slightly higher bandwidth under ideal conditions

problem: Maintaining adequate gate coupling ratio and compensating FG-to-FG coupling interference for scaling virtual-ground multilevel-cell flash memory.
method: Novel bowl-shaped floating-gate structure, segmented virtual-ground architecture with bit-line isolation, and two-step programming with channel hot electron injection Vth compensation.
finding: Achieved GCR of 0.6, near-complete elimination of Vth shift in word-line direction, and successful implementation of a 3F²/bit test chip with good reliability.
dataset: Virtual-ground MLC FG flash memory test chip
metric: gate coupling ratio (GCR), Vth shift, cell size (F²/bit)
result: GCR = 0.6, cell size = 3F²/bit, Vth shift nearly eliminated in WL direction

problem: Investigating how 3D virtual reality can improve high school students' mental models of biological cells compared to traditional 2D learning materials.
method: Inquiry-based 3D VR game with pre/post drawing analysis and biology assessments to evaluate changes in students' mental models and factual knowledge.
finding: Students' post-drawings showed more organelle types, increased density, and additional complexity, indicating more accurate mental models, but factual knowledge scores did not improve.
dataset: High school biology students (sample size not specified)
metric: organelle types in drawings, organelle density, factual knowledge test scores
result: improved mental models (more organelle types/density) but no factual knowledge improvement

problem: Enabling virtual commissioning of linked manufacturing cells from different manufacturers with material flow in a 3D integration platform.
method: Three approaches using NVIDIA Omniverse: (1) source/sink with OPC UA, (2) bidirectional connector with Apache Kafka, (3) bidirectional connector using platform extension only.
finding: Virtual commissioning of multiple linked digital twins from different manufacturers with material flow is feasible in a 3D platform, contributing significantly to the industrial metaverse.
dataset: Linked manufacturing cells with digital twins from different manufacturers

problem: Analyzing performance of dual virtual cell systems under overlay convergent networks with cognitive networking.
method: STTC-based dual virtual cell (DVC) system with radio resource monitoring; simulation analysis of imperfect CSI effects on performance.
finding: DVC employment strategy based on resource monitoring improves multi-user high-rate data transmission; quantitative analysis reveals the impact of imperfect CSI on system performance.
dataset: Simulated overlay convergent network with cognitive networking
metric: spectral efficiency, effect of imperfect CSI

problem: Concept, design and operation of periodic virtual cell manufacturing (P-VCM).
Transferring cell type labels between single-cell RNA-seq datasets with batch effects across platforms and species.
method: scAdapt: virtual adversarial domain adaptation network that trains an enhanced classifier using labeled source and unlabeled target data, aligning source and pseudo-labeled target centroids for joint embedding.
finding: scAdapt outperforms existing methods for cross-platform, cross-species, spatial transcriptomic, and COVID-19 immune dataset classification, with superior cell mixing and preserved cluster structure.
dataset: Simulated, cross-platform, cross-species, spatial transcriptomic, and COVID-19 immune scRNA-seq datasets
metric: classification accuracy, cell mixing, cluster structure preservation
baseline: existing supervised cell type classification methods
result: outperforms existing methods across all tested scenarios

problem: Minimizing energy consumption of user equipment in virtualized small cell networks with mobile edge computing under multiple mobile virtual network operators.
method: Joint offloading, time slice, and power allocation formulated as mixed integer nonlinear programming; solved via biconvex decomposition with ADMM-based distributed algorithm.
finding: The ADMM-based distributed algorithm achieves performance close to optimal with higher network performance and better adaptation than tabular multi-agent RL.
dataset: Simulated virtualized small cell network with MEC
metric: grid energy consumption, traffic drop rate
result: performance close to optimal, superior to MRL benchmark

problem: Developing a virtual aluminum reduction cell simulation.
Simulating virtual cathode oscillator (vircator) behavior including self-consistent anode foil ablation effects on microwave output.
method: 2D and 3D relativistic electromagnetic particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations with self-consistent anode foil dynamics, plasma expansion, and AK gap closure modeling.
finding: Microwave output power initially increases then decreases as plasma fills the AK gap (~230 ns, consistent with ~2 cm/μs expansion); dominant output frequency varies from 6.3 to 7.6 GHz; foil damage is higher near axis with rapid erosion phase.
dataset: Two vircator designs from literature, validated with locally developed PIC codes
metric: microwave output power, dominant frequency, foil damage profile
result: frequency shift 6.3-7.6 GHz, gap closure at ~230 ns consistent with 2 cm/μs plasma velocity

problem: Understanding how heterogeneous myoendothelial gap junction (MEGJ) distribution affects electrical cell-cell communication and flow control in arterioles.
method: Computational modeling of virtual arterioles varying number, conductance, and distribution of MEGJs to simulate electrical conduction between endothelial and smooth muscle cell layers.
finding: A minimal number of randomly distributed MEGJs optimizes arteriolar cell-cell communication; heterogeneous MEGJ distributions enable apparently non-decaying conduction in the SMC layer without regeneration.
dataset: Virtual arteriole model with endothelial and smooth muscle cell layers
metric: conduction efficiency, membrane potential responses
result: minimal heterogeneous MEGJs outperform assumed high uniform density for conduction

problem: Minimizing grid energy consumption and traffic drop rate in virtualized small cells powered by energy harvesters with opportunistic baseband offloading.
method: Distributed deep reinforcement learning (DDRL) with inter-vSC coordination via battery state information exchange for functional split control.
finding: DDRL with coordination achieves performance close to optimal, outperforming tabular multi-agent RL in grid energy consumption and traffic drop rate with better environmental adaptation.
dataset: Simulated network of virtualized small cells with energy harvesters
metric: grid energy consumption, traffic drop rate, cost savings
result: performance close to optimal, superior to MRL benchmark

problem: Modeling tumour growth prior to chemo-radiotherapy starting from a single stem cell to support treatment enhancement studies.
method: Monte Carlo simulation using biological parameters including cell type ratios, cell cycle phases, Gaussian-distributed cycle times (mean 33h), and natural cell loss.
finding: The model accurately reflects exponential cell distribution along cell cycle (70% G1, 15% S, 10% G2, 5% M), maintains biological cell proportions (2% stem, 13% proliferating, 85% nonproliferating), and R=3 ratio for clinically detectable to lethal size is consistent with biological data.
dataset: Simulated head and neck tumour growth
metric: cell cycle distribution, cell proportion ratios, growth ratio R (detectable to lethal)
result: R=3, 70/15/10/5% cell cycle distribution, 2/13/85% cell type proportions — matching literature

problem: Understanding the cellular and molecular basis of innate immune responses in the central nervous system and their outcomes.
method: Review of CNS innate immunity mediated by resident microglia and astrocytes, including cross-talk with infiltrating T cells and innate immune components.
finding: CNS immune reactions often occur in virtual isolation from peripheral innate/adaptive interplay; outcomes (infection resolution, neurodegeneration, or neural repair) depend on context of microglia-astrocyte-T cell interactions.

problem: Determining the cytokine requirements for virtual memory CD8+ T cells across different mouse genetic backgrounds.
method: Comparison of VM cell dependence on IL-4 and IL-15 in C57BL/6 vs BALB/c mice, using CD1d-deficient mice and infection models.
finding: VM cells in C57BL/6 rely on IL-15 while BALB/c VM cells rely on IL-4 from NKT cells; this cytokine-mediated control of precursor populations prior to infection influences subsequent virus-specific CD8+ T cell memory.
dataset: C57BL/6 and BALB/c mice, CD1d-deficient mice
metric: VM cell frequency, effector and memory T cell responses
result: BALB/c VM cells rely on IL-4/NKT axis; C57BL/6 on IL-15

problem: Testing whether the contractile agonist angiotensin II (AII) regulates smooth muscle cell growth via hypertrophy or hyperplasia.
method: Treatment of quiescent and subconfluent cultured rat aortic smooth muscle cells with AII; measurement of protein content, protein synthesis/degradation, DNA content, and cell cycle analysis.
finding: AII induces hypertrophy (not hyperplasia) in smooth muscle cells, increasing protein synthesis within 6-9 hours; effect is maximal at 1 μM (ED50 5 nM) in postconfluent and as low as 0.1 nM in subconfluent cultures, with 50% increase in 4C DNA cells suggesting G2 arrest or tetraploidy.
dataset: cultured rat aortic smooth muscle cells
metric: protein content increase, ED50, DNA content (4C fraction)
result: 20% protein increase (postconfluent), 38% (subconfluent); ED50 = 5 nM; 50% increase in 4C DNA cells

problem: Understanding how CD8+ T cells sense and restrict the HIV DNA reservoir in ART-treated patients.
method: Correlation analysis of virtual memory CD8+ T cells (TVM: CD45RA+, PanKIR+/NKG2A+) with HIV DNA levels, cytokine profiling, cytotoxic assays, and ex vivo HIV reactivation suppression system.
finding: TVM cells negatively correlate with HIV DNA reservoir, constitutively express cytotoxic granules, suppress HIV reactivation through KIR-mediated recognition via both cytolytic and noncytolytic mechanisms.
dataset: HIV-1-infected patients with effective ART
metric: correlation with HIV DNA levels, IFN-α2 and IL-15 levels, HIV reactivation suppression
result: TVM cells negatively correlate with HIV DNA, positively with IFN-α2 and IL-15

problem: Analyzing reverse-link and forward-link capacity in CDMA distributed antenna systems with macrodiversity.
method: Analytical outage probability derivation for MRC-based macrodiversity with virtual cell concept; proposed new transmission scheme with optimal power allocation for forward-link.
finding: Reverse-link capacity increases significantly with macrodiversity; forward-link capacity cannot increase with simulcasting regardless of power allocation, but the proposed new scheme achieves rapid capacity increase with more distributed antennas.
dataset: Simulated CDMA distributed antenna system
metric: outage probability, link capacity
baseline: simulcasting with various power allocation schemes
result: significant reverse-link capacity increase; forward-link capacity increase with proposed scheme

problem: Inducing pacemaker activity in virtual mammalian ventricular cells.
Finding allosteric EGFR inhibitors to overcome T790M/C797S triple mutation-mediated drug resistance in non-small cell lung cancer.
method: Structure-based virtual screening targeting the allosteric pocket of C797S EGFR tyrosine kinase, followed by 10 ns molecular dynamics simulation and in silico ADMET prediction.
finding: ZINC20531199 identified as a stable allosteric inhibitor binding away from the ATP-binding site, with pharmacokinetic predictions within human use range.
dataset: EGFR T790M/C797S tyrosine kinase crystal structure, ZINC database
metric: docking score, MD stability (10 ns), ADMET predictions
result: ZINC20531199 stable in allosteric pocket over 10 ns MD; ADMET within range

problem: Discovering small-molecule ERBB3 inhibitors for NSCLC driven by CD74-NRG1 fusion-mediated ERBB3/PI3K-AKT signaling.
method: Sequential virtual screening (pharmacophore and docking) of natural product and Chinese medicine databases against ERBB3 crystal structure, followed by in vitro validation in A549 cells.
finding: Five of six selected compounds significantly inhibited A549 cell growth; compound VS1 showed IC50 of 269.75 μM, comparable to nimustine hydrochloride positive control (264.14 μM).
dataset: ERBB3 crystal structure, natural products and Chinese medicine databases, A549 lung cancer cells
metric: IC50 (cell growth inhibition), ADMET predictions
result: VS1 IC50 = 269.75 μM; 5/6 compounds active

problem: Constructing a virtual reality simulation environment for robotic manufacturing cells from scratch.
method: Semantic robot model with inverse kinematics for 6R arms, bounding-box collision detection, interactive trajectory planning; implemented in C++ with open 3D graphics using VRML models from CAD.
finding: The system supports enhanced desktop and hybrid wall setups for design and training of robotic manufacturing cells.
dataset: 6R robotic arm tending a turning centre

problem: Determining whether grid cells exist in the human brain and identifying their distribution across brain regions.
method: fMRI during virtual reality spatial navigation, looking for speed-modulated six-fold rotational symmetry in running direction predicted from rat single-unit electrophysiology.
finding: Grid-cell-like representations found in entorhinal/subicular, posterior/medial parietal, lateral temporal, and medial prefrontal areas; strongest in right entorhinal cortex, with directional signal coherence correlating with spatial memory performance.
dataset: Human fMRI during virtual reality environment navigation
metric: six-fold rotational symmetry signal, spatial memory performance correlation
baseline: rat entorhinal cortex grid cell electrophysiology
result: grid-cell-like signal found; entorhinal coherence correlates with spatial memory

problem: Developing a comprehensive 3D model of hair cell mechanoelectrical transduction incorporating gating spring theory.
method: Virtual hair cell combining full 3D bundle mechanical model with gating spring theory; tip link tension and fast adaptation modulator kinetics determine individual channel opening/closing.
finding: The model reproduces three in vitro features: transient bundle rebound during fast adaptation, minimized dynamic stiffness at steepest I-X curve, and directional polarity of activation.
dataset: Turtle utricle hair cells (in vitro data for validation)
metric: bundle tip displacement, dynamic stiffness, I-X curve slope, polarity response
result: reproduces transient rebound, stiffness minimum at steepest I-X, and polarity

problem: Developing a call admission control scheme for ATM networks that estimates cell loss quality for bursty traffic sources.
method: Virtual cell loss probability estimation using peak and mean rate parameters, combined with traffic clustering and subcapacity assignment; virtual bandwidth method for practical implementation.
finding: The approach effectively estimates real cell loss probability for heterogeneous/homogeneous traffic when burst length exceeds buffer capacity, enabling QoS-aware call admission control.
dataset: ATM network traffic models (heterogeneous and homogeneous)
metric: virtual cell loss probability, quality of service (QoS)

problem: Characterizing the molecular profile and virtual karyotype of clear cell papillary renal cell carcinoma (CCP-RCC) to understand its genetic basis.
method: Targeted next-generation sequencing and single-nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) array on FFPE tissue from 22 CCP-RCC cases.
finding: Found 30 somatic variants across 63.3% of cases (56.7% predicted deleterious), and copy number abnormalities/LOH in 22.7%; CCP-RCCs are genetically heterogeneous, with some resembling clear cell RCC.
dataset: 22 CCP-RCC cases from University of Alabama at Birmingham
metric: somatic variant count, deleterious prediction rate, CNA/LOH frequency
baseline: clear cell RCC and papillary RCC molecular profiles
result: 30 variants in 63.3% of cases; 22.7% with CNA/LOH; genetically heterogeneous

problem: Assessing student and teacher perceptions of virtual laboratory simulations (Labster) in undergraduate cell biology education.
method: Surveys and interviews with 352 respondents evaluating user-friendliness, motivation, autonomy, and suitability as lab replacement or preparation.
finding: >90% found VLs user-friendly and visually engaging; >80% reported increased motivation and confidence; >90% preferred VLs as pre-lab preparation rather than full replacement; ~60% noted significant technical resource requirements.
dataset: 352 first-year undergraduate cell biology students and teachers
metric: perception survey scores (user-friendliness, motivation, autonomy, confidence)
result: >90% user-friendly, >80% increased motivation, 72% view as potential replacement, >90% prefer as pre-lab

problem: Evaluating the effect of mechanoelectric transduction parameters on virtual hair cell response.
method: Parametric simulation varying gating threshold, critical tension, resting tension, Ca2+ concentration, and number of forced cilia in the 3D virtual hair cell model.
finding: Gating threshold dominates initial sensitivity; critical tension affects time course and F-X relationship but not peak response; higher Ca2+ reduces fast adaptation time constant; resting tension helps overcome viscous drag during oscillatory movement.
dataset: Virtual hair cell model (turtle utricle)
metric: initial sensitivity, peak response (I), time course I(t), F-X relationship, fast adaptation time constant
result: gating threshold dominates sensitivity; Ca2+ reduces adaptation time constant

problem: Validating robotic cell layout design and conducting implementation planning using virtual reality before building the real cell.
method: Development of a semi-immersive virtual environment for a robotic cell with 1:1 scale digital objects.
finding: The VR environment provides users with a sense of presence and enables working with digital objects as in the real world, suitable for layout validation, implementation planning, teaching, and training.
dataset: Robotic manufacturing cell

problem: Whether CD4+ T cells can acquire memory or regulatory phenotypes without antigen encounter, challenging the classical model of antigen-driven T cell maturation.
method: TCR sequencing and repertoire analysis of highly purified CD4+ T cell populations (naïve, regulatory, central memory, effector memory) from naïve mice, using HEL as model antigen.
finding: Antigen-inexperienced CD4+ T cells reside in naïve, regulatory, and memory compartments at similar frequencies without clonal expansion, and pathogenic clones are repetitively regenerated throughout adulthood, arguing against T cell-depleting therapies for autoimmunity.
dataset: Mouse CD4+ T cells, HEL-specific clones, aged vs young mice
metric: Clone frequency across T cell compartments, TCR sequence identity
result: HEL-inexperienced clones found at similar frequencies across all four T cell compartments; majority unexpanded

problem: Developing a predictive model for antiestrogenic activity and discovering novel MCF-7 breast cancer cell line inhibitors.
method: 3D-QSAR model using pharmacophore-based alignment (Schrödinger Phase), followed by virtual screening of 3D databases and in vitro validation against MCF-7 cells.
finding: A 5-feature pharmacophore yielded a predictive 3D-QSAR model, and 11 of 14 virtually screened compounds showed inhibitory activity against MCF-7 cells.
dataset: MCF-7 human breast adenocarcinoma cell line, external test set of 21 compounds
metric: q², r², IC50
result: Test set q²=0.73, external r²=0.69, 11/14 hits active with IC50 30-186 μM

problem: Developing a specific small-molecule inhibitor of Rac GTPase to selectively block its activation by GEF without affecting related GTPases Cdc42 or RhoA.
method: Structure-based virtual screening targeting a Rac1 surface groove critical for GEF specification, followed by in vitro binding/activation assays and cell-based functional studies.
finding: NSC23766 selectively inhibits Rac1 activation by Trio/Tiam1 in a dose-dependent manner without affecting Cdc42 or RhoA, and suppresses proliferation, anchorage-independent growth, and invasion in prostate cancer PC-3 cells.
dataset: Rac1 GTPase, human prostate cancer PC-3 cells
metric: Dose-dependent inhibition, cell proliferation, anchorage-independent growth, invasion
baseline: Closely related GTPases Cdc42 and RhoA (selectivity controls)
result: Selective Rac1 inhibition; no interference with Cdc42/RhoA binding or activation

problem: How to empower biomedical discovery by designing AI agent systems capable of skeptical learning and reasoning that integrate AI models with experimental platforms.
method: Perspective/review proposing collaborative AI agents using large language models, generative models, structured memory, and ML tools for biomedical research workflows.
finding: AI agents can impact virtual cell simulation, phenotype control, cellular circuit design, and therapy development by combining human creativity with AI's analytical capabilities.

problem: Reducing complexity in programming heterogeneous manufacturing equipment by creating a unified programming platform.
method: Virtual reality-based two-way digital twin with a common HMI for programming all robots in a manufacturing cell, implemented as a cyber-physical system.
finding: VR-based digital twin enables simulation, testing, and safety checks before program deployment, providing a common programming interface for diverse manufacturing equipment.
dataset: Industrial robot manufacturing cell setup

problem: Measuring the psychological effect of an art intervention (virtual window) on quality of life in hospitalized patients undergoing stem cell transplantation.
method: Prospective randomized trial (N=199) comparing art intervention group vs control, measuring anxiety, depression, and stress via HADS and Distress Thermometer.
finding: The Open Window intervention significantly reduced anxiety and depression before and after transplant and improved patients' reported experiences.
dataset: 199 stem cell transplant patients for hematological malignancy (96 intervention, 103 control)
metric: HADS (anxiety, depression), Distress Thermometer, p-value
result: Anxiety reduction: p=0.001 (pre-transplant), p=0.041 (day 7), p=0.035 (day 60); depression p=0.022

problem: Reducing deployment costs of small cells in dense traffic areas by using large antenna arrays at macrocells as an alternative to physical small cell deployment.
method: Virtual Small Cells (VSCs) created by activating multiple antenna elements at macrocells to form directive beams, replacing picocells.
finding: VSCs are reconfigurable to traffic fluctuation, increase throughput by 50% over macro-only deployment, and reduce OPEX/CAPEX by eliminating physical small cell infrastructure.
dataset: 4G dense traffic area simulation
metric: Throughput, network power consumption, OPEX/CAPEX
result: 50% throughput increase, reduced global network power consumption

problem: Reviewing the physiological functions of plant cell walls in growth and environmental responses.
method: Editorial/virtual issue introducing published articles on cell wall functions across embryogenesis, vegetative growth, reproductive growth, and environmental responses.
finding: Cell walls have multiple functions including determining cell shape/size, cell-cell adhesion, controlling differentiation and growth, and promoting stress tolerance.
dataset: Plant cell wall studies (Journal of Plant Research collection)

problem: Enabling cellular access multi-tenancy through small cell base station virtualization to handle growing mobile traffic and standard diversity.
method: Virtualized small cell base station with multiple isolated LTE PHY stacks running on a hypervisor on off-the-shelf x86 hardware with shared radio head.
finding: Multiple virtualized base stations achieve throughput equal or close to theoretical maximum, with full stack at the edge eliminating the need for low-latency high-throughput fronthaul required by C-RAN.
dataset: LTE PHY stack virtualization testbed
metric: Throughput vs theoretical maximum
result: Throughput equal or close to theoretical maximum

problem: Integrating cell formation, scheduling, and facility layout decisions in virtual manufacturing cells with assembly aspects.
method: Mathematical model minimizing material handling costs and cycle time, solved with Population-based Simulated Annealing (PSA) combined with linear programming.
finding: PSA showed superior performance compared to CPLEX solver and standard simulated annealing for concurrent scheduling and layout optimization.
dataset: Manufacturing cell case study, literature benchmark instances
metric: Material handling costs, cycle time, computation time
result: PSA outperformed CPLEX and standard SA on benchmark instances

problem: Evaluating whether low-energy (40-keV) virtual monochromatic dual-energy CT images improve visualization of head and neck squamous cell carcinoma compared to single-energy CT.
method: Comparison of 60 dual-energy CT and 60 SECT scans with objective (absolute/relative attenuation) and subjective evaluation by 6 radiologists.
finding: 40-keV VMIs significantly improved tumor visibility both objectively and subjectively, with substantial to almost perfect interrater agreement.
dataset: 120 CT scans (60 DECT, 60 SECT) from 2 institutions, head and neck SCC patients
metric: Absolute/relative attenuation, interrater agreement (κ), p-value
result: Higher attenuation on 40-keV VMIs (p<0.0001), κ=0.61-1.0 interrater agreement

problem: Developing a production engineering-oriented virtual factory using a planning cell-based approach for manufacturing systems design.
Designing a scalable routing algorithm for the mobile subsystem of next-generation tactical communications systems.
method: Virtual cell layout (VCL) based dynamic source routing (VB-DSR) combining ad hoc and cellular infrastructureless architecture for tactical communications.
finding: VB-DSR is scalable for tactical communications, enabling 3G personal communications services through a hybrid ad hoc/cellular architecture.
dataset: Tactical communications simulation
metric: Scalability performance
result: Scalable performance for tactical communications

problem: Helping learners visualize the human cell division process (mitosis and meiosis) which is difficult to understand from traditional teaching methods.
method: Interactive mobile VR application (InCell VR) developed in Unity with mini games, evaluated via pre/post-tests and usability study.
finding: Students using the VR application showed positive knowledge gains and reported the app as useful, easy to use, and satisfying.
dataset: Biology learners (pre- and post-test evaluation)
metric: Knowledge gain (pre/post-test), usability scores
result: Positive knowledge gains; high usability ratings

problem: Building and testing complex hypotheses about intracellular processes by applying disciplined computational approaches that explicitly treat spatial complexity.
method: Review of computational cell biology tools with emphasis on Virtual Cell software, covering deterministic and stochastic simulations of spatiotemporal cellular events.
finding: General-purpose computational tools like Virtual Cell enable cell biologists to build spatial models and generate simulations, with applications demonstrated for RNA trafficking and neuronal calcium dynamics.
dataset: RNA trafficking, neuronal calcium dynamics (illustrative examples)

problem: Understanding how the convergence of mobile communications and computing drives social transformation.
method: Sociological and anthropological analysis of emerging mobile technology adoption across global cities (Tokyo, Helsinki, Manhattan, Manila).
finding: Mobile communications enable fundamentally new forms of collective action ('smart mobs'), with implications ranging from political activism to privacy threats.

problem: Investigating whether diatom frustules (silicified cell walls) provide mechanical protection and contribute to evolutionary success.
method: Real and virtual loading tests using calibrated glass microneedles and finite element analysis on centric and pennate diatom cells.
finding: Diatom frustules are remarkably strong due to their architecture and silica material properties, concluding they evolved as mechanical armor against predators.
dataset: Centric and pennate diatom cells
metric: Force required to break frustules
result: Exceptional force required to break frustules

problem: Reviewing the potential of Punica granatum (pomegranate) for prevention and treatment of inflammation and cancer.
method: Comprehensive literature review of pharmacological activities across all pomegranate anatomical compartments (seed, juice, peel, leaf, flower, bark, roots).
finding: Pomegranate components possess antioxidant, weakly estrogenic, and anticancer activities including interference with tumor cell proliferation, cell cycle, invasion, and angiogenesis, associated with anti-inflammatory effects.
dataset: Punica granatum (pomegranate) phytochemical and pharmacological literature
result: 7x increase in Medline publications over prior period

problem: Comparing electronic structure calculation approaches for doped BaFe2As2 iron-based superconductor materials.
dataset: Doped BaFe2As2 materials
baseline: Virtual crystal approximation vs super-cell approach

problem: Deriving a more accurate equation for calculating interparticle spacing (IPS) in colloidal systems, which is critical for estimating suspension viscosity.
method: Mathematical derivation based on virtual cell concept (free volume per particle, originated by Kuwabara), validated against experimental titania data.
finding: The new IPS equation gives reasonable values including cases where the Dinger-Funk equation produced anomalous results.
dataset: Three commercial sources of titania
metric: IPS values, comparison with experimental data
result: Reasonable IPS values where Dinger-Funk gave anomalous results

problem: Discovering small-molecule inhibitors of immune cell checkpoints as alternatives to monoclonal antibodies for immunotherapy across cancers and other diseases.
method: High-throughput virtual screening of 9,466 compounds against 7 immune checkpoints using docking, MM-GBSA, ADME filtering, and MD simulation.
finding: Rutin hydrate (targeting all 7 ICPs), amikacin hydrate (6 ICPs), and 6-hydroxyluteolin (3 ICPs) were identified as best immune checkpoint inhibitors.
dataset: 3 small-molecule libraries (9,466 compounds), 7 immune cell checkpoints
metric: Docking score, MM-GBSA, ADME properties, MD simulation stability
result: 13 ICP inhibitors identified; top 3 compounds targeting 3-7 checkpoints each

problem: Developing methodology for preventive ergonomic analysis of assembly work-cells in virtual environments before physical implementation.
method: Digital Human Models (DHM) and Virtual Reality tools for simulating manual assembly tasks, with adapted ergonomics evaluation methods for virtual environments.
finding: Virtual simulation with digital manikins enables early-stage ergonomic and feasibility analysis, suggesting solutions for more ergonomic and efficient assembly tasks.
dataset: Automotive assembly case study
metric: Ergonomics evaluation (musculoskeletal risk), task feasibility
result: New solutions identified for more ergonomic and efficient tasks

problem: Dynamically selecting UE-based virtual small cell base stations to handle non-stationary, non-uniform mobile traffic distribution in 5G networks.
method: Affinity Propagation Clustering to select optimal UE-VBSs, with modified message passing using received power as parameter; evaluated in MATLAB and NS3.
finding: The technique effectively selects UE-VBSs to supplement loaded SBSs, though scalability limitations are identified and addressed through modified message passing.
dataset: 5G network simulation (MATLAB and NS3)
metric: Clustering performance, scalability
baseline: Static small cell deployment (Femto, Pico, Nano cells)

problem: Comparing target localization accuracy between conventional X-ray simulation and CT-based virtual simulation for palliative radiotherapy in non-small-cell lung cancer.
method: Randomized double-blind trial (N=86) comparing conventional simulator fields with digitally reconstructed radiographs from CT data.
finding: CT-based virtual simulation allows more accurate target definition, reducing geographical misses and treatment-related toxicity.
dataset: 86 NSCLC patients undergoing palliative radiotherapy
metric: Field area mismatch, target coverage, normal tissue over-coverage
result: Major mismatch in 66.2%; conventional fields 24.5% larger; target under-coverage 16.4%; normal tissue over-coverage 25.4%

problem: Developing InGaP single-junction solar cells on Ge-on-Si virtual substrates for cost-effective solar power conversion.
method: MOCVD growth on lattice-matched Ge-on-Si virtual substrates, optoelectronic simulation, and EBIC measurements of threading dislocation density.
finding: InGaP solar cells on Ge-on-Si virtual substrates achieve comparable Voc to those on Ge substrates when TDD < 2×10⁶ cm⁻², with estimated efficiencies up to 34.3%.
dataset: InGaP solar cells on Ge-on-Si and Ge substrates
metric: Voc, TDD, efficiency, carrier lifetime
baseline: InGaP solar cells on bulk Ge substrate
result: Voc 0.96-1.43 V at ~0.5 Sun; efficiency 16.6%-34.3% for 1.9-2.4 eV spectrum

problem: Integrating AI technologies (large language models, virtual cell simulations) to decipher complex multi-target mechanisms of Traditional Chinese Medicine for stroke treatment.
method: Review proposing theoretical frameworks combining AI with TCM research for both basic and clinical studies.
finding: AI can overcome traditional limitations in TCM research, enabling more precise mechanistic understanding and TCM-based stroke treatments.
dataset: TCM stroke treatment literature

problem: Scheduling jobs in virtual manufacturing cells with lot-streaming to minimize makespan, considering machine assignments, starting times, and sub-lot sizes.
method: Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) formulation with a Genetic Algorithm (GA) using a novel chromosome structure for VMC environments.
finding: The GA obtains optimal solutions when sub-lot number equals 1 and performs favorably across a wide range of problem instances.
dataset: VMC scheduling benchmark instances
metric: Makespan, optimality gap, computation time
result: GA obtains optimum for all instances with sub-lot number=1

problem: Creating a fine virtual electrode using electron beam for mechanical strain microscopy of living cells.
dataset: Living cells

problem: Visualizing complex protein interaction networks, complexes, and 3D structures in a biologically meaningful cellular context.
method: The Interactorium tool uses a Virtual Cell as context for seamless visualization, with algorithms to reduce complexity via putative complex generation and protein 'twins' for multi-location proteins.
finding: The tool can display networks of up to 40,000 proteins or 6,000 complexes with real-time data manipulation, enabling multi-level viewing of cellular molecular biology.
dataset: Saccharomyces cerevisiae protein interaction network
metric: Network scale (up to 40,000 proteins, 6,000 complexes)
result: Handles 40,000 proteins or 6,000 complexes with real-time manipulation

problem: Developing maskless fabrication of light-directed oligonucleotide microarrays to replace costly photolithographic masks.
method: Maskless array synthesizer (MAS) using digital micromirror array to generate virtual masks with 1:1 reflective UV imaging on glass substrate in a flow cell reaction chamber.
finding: MAS synthesized microarrays with >76,000 features (16 μm²) at high yield, readily discriminating single-base pair mismatches after hybridization.
dataset: Oligonucleotide microarrays (>76,000 features)
metric: Feature count, feature size, repetitive yield, single-base mismatch discrimination
result: >76,000 features at 16 μm², high repetitive yield, single-bp mismatch discrimination

problem: Mapping the optoelectronic property space of organic dyes and screening for dyes suitable for dye-sensitized water splitting and solar cells.
method: High-throughput virtual screening of 45,000 diketopyrrolopyrrole (DPP) dyes for optoelectronic properties.
finding: Property space mapping identified DPP dyes suitable for dye-sensitized proton reduction and dye-sensitized solar cells.
dataset: 45,000 diketopyrrolopyrrole dyes
metric: Optoelectronic properties
result: 45,000 dyes screened

problem: Rapidly and cheaply prototyping and optimizing novel nano-scale solar cell designs through computational simulation.
method: FDTD simulations coupled with particle swarm optimization (PSO) for thin film silicon plasmonic and photonic crystal organic solar cells.
finding: Optical enhancements of approximately 15% can be achieved with optimization requiring only a few hundred simulations (hours on a workstation).
dataset: Thin film silicon plasmonic solar cells, photonic crystal organic solar cells
metric: Optical enhancement percentage, simulation count
result: ~15% optical enhancement, few hundred simulations in a few hours

problem: Developing a tractable analytical model for virtualized downlink cellular networks with ultra-dense small cells using C-plane/U-plane splitting.
method: Stochastic geometry-based analytical framework for modeling virtualized RAN with control/user plane separation.
finding: The model provides design insights for virtualized RANs and quantifies performance improvement from RAN virtualization in ultra-dense deployments.
dataset: Ultra-dense small cell network simulation
metric: SINR, spatial spectral efficiency, network throughput

problem: Comparing learning gains and self-efficacy between traditional live laboratory and virtual computer simulation experiences for cell and molecular biology concepts.
method: Laboratory module using S. cerevisiae mating pathway with live experiments and Unity-based computer simulations; assessed via pre/post knowledge tests and attitudinal surveys.
finding: Students performing simulation alone or simulation plus live lab demonstrated learning gains (greater for live lab), while students performing neither did not; both modalities increased engagement and self-efficacy.
dataset: Undergraduate biology students, Saccharomyces cerevisiae mating pathway
metric: Learning gains (pre/post-test), self-efficacy, engagement
result: Significant learning gains for simulation and live lab groups; greater gains for live lab

problem: Visualizing mitochondria in living cells without fluorescent markers using computational approaches.
method: Physics-guided deep learning with adversarial neural network integrating microscope point spread function for virtual labeling from bright-field images.
finding: The approach achieves significantly better virtual labeling of mitochondria than state-of-the-art, enabling segmentation and tracking previously possible only with fluorescent labels.
dataset: Living cell bright-field and fluorescence microscopy images
metric: Pearson correlation
result: Average Pearson correlation 0.86 vs SOTA 0.71

problem: Designing an interactive virtual environment for modeling factories using cellular manufacturing methodologies.
method: Open-architecture virtual factory with Modified Boolean Method clustering algorithm for automatic cell formation and 3D visualization.
finding: The virtual factory makes cellular manufacturing design intuitive, enabling better optimization for maintainability and performance that would not be visible without 3D visualization.
dataset: Manufacturing cell formation problem instances
metric: Productivity, human factors, ergonomics
result: Automatic generation of complex virtual manufacturing environments

problem: Investigating the in vivo function of BMP-7 using gene knockout in mice.
method: Generation of BMP-7-deficient mice using embryonic stem cell technology, with histological analysis and in situ hybridization at multiple developmental stages.
finding: BMP-7 is essential for kidney development (nephrogenesis inducer) — deficient mice show virtual absence of glomeruli — and also required for eye development and skeletal patterning.
dataset: BMP-7-deficient mice, embryonic stages 12.5-14.5 dpc
metric: Histological analysis, Pax-2 and Wnt-4 expression
result: Virtual absence of glomeruli in newborn kidneys; affected Pax-2/Wnt-4 expression at 12.5-14.5 dpc

problem: Designing virtual manufacturing cells with optimized machine sharing to improve utilization and respond to demand fluctuations.
method: Two-stage mathematical approach: Stage 1 optimizes number of part families, Stage 2 uses novel formulation with new variables defining shared machines in virtual cells.
finding: The proposed method accurately defines shared machine numbers and was validated in a real plant, demonstrating practicability.
dataset: Manufacturing plant case study
metric: Machine utilization, number of shared machines
result: Validated in plant application; improved accuracy of shared machine definition

problem: Understanding how multisensory cues beyond vision and self-motion contribute to hippocampal place cell spatial coding.
method: Comparison of hippocampal place cell activity in rats running in immersive virtual reality versus real-world environments.
finding: Twice as many neurons were active in the real world compared to VR; place cells encoded position in the real world but encoded distance in virtual reality.
dataset: Rat hippocampal place cells, virtual reality vs real-world navigation
metric: Number of active neurons, spatial vs distance encoding
result: 2x more active neurons in real world; VR place cells encode distance instead of position

problem: Identifying security challenges of small cell as a service in virtualized mobile edge computing environments.
Minimizing energy consumption of energy-harvesting virtual small cells by dynamically controlling functional splits between local processing and edge cloud.
method: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (distributed Fuzzy Q-Learning and Q-Learning) for dynamic functional split control in Cloud RAN architecture.
finding: Coordinated multi-agent RL via broadcasting system-level information achieves higher cumulative rewards closer to offline bounds than uncoordinated solutions, with continuous state/action enabling faster convergence.
dataset: Simulated dense small cell network with energy harvesters
metric: Energy minimization, cumulative reward, convergence speed
baseline: Off-line optimization bounds, uncoordinated learning agents
result: Coordinated agents achieve rewards closer to off-line bounds; faster convergence with continuous representation

problem: Designing an effective emergency broadcast protocol for sensor-based inter-vehicle communications on highways.
method: Geographical routing-based protocol dividing highway into virtual cells with dynamically elected cell reflectors for message prioritization and forwarding; evaluated by simulation.
finding: The proposed protocol is more effective than existing inter-vehicle protocols for emergency message dissemination.
dataset: Highway inter-vehicle communication simulation
metric: Broadcast effectiveness
result: More effective than existing protocols

problem: Incorporating spatial features (cell shape, compartment sizes, molecule distribution) into mechanistic mathematical models of cell signaling.
method: Virtual Cell software for spatial modeling with deterministic and stochastic formulations; reaction-diffusion numerical methods with geometry specification.
finding: Spatial modeling provides insights for interpreting experimental results, demonstrated through cAMP signaling in neurons.
dataset: cAMP signaling in neurons (case study)

problem: Improving mortality prognostication for critically ill pediatric allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplant patients requiring PICU admission.
method: Merged Virtual Pediatric Systems and CIBMTR databases; evaluated 25 early risk factors using multivariable modeling for PICU mortality prediction in 936 patients.
finding: Five independent risk factors were identified; top-quartile risk patients had 3x greater mortality than others, with classification accuracy of 75.2%.
dataset: 936 critically ill pediatric allogeneic HCT patients, 1,532 PICU admissions (2009-2014)
metric: PICU mortality rate, classification accuracy, 95% CI
result: Overall PICU mortality 17.4%; top quartile 35.1% vs 11.5% (p<0.001); classification accuracy 75.2%

problem: Developing a method for automated drivability calibration earlier in vehicle development without requiring mature hardware.
method: Model-based calibration using real-time physics-based engine/vehicle simulation with real engine controller hardware in a virtual engine test cell, combined with online optimization.
finding: Automated drivability calibration in a virtual test cell environment is feasible, with accurate engine simulation under steady-state and transient conditions sensitive to calibration changes.
dataset: Engine simulation model (steady-state and transient conditions)
metric: Simulation accuracy, sensitivity to calibration changes
baseline: Traditional vehicle drivability calibration on mature hardware
result: Feasible automated calibration; compresses concept-to-production timeline

problem: Addressing the statistical and computational challenges inherent in building whole cell models.
How to introduce and teach systems biology methods for understanding complex cellular systems involving hundreds or thousands of gene products.
method: Textbook covering modeling methods including synthetic circuits, transcriptional, signaling, metabolic, and integrated multi-network models.
finding: Provides a pedagogical framework progressing from synthetic biological circuits to whole-cell models, building intuition about strengths and weaknesses of each modeling method.

problem: Developing a mathematical framework for modeling biological cells from a physicochemical perspective, including self-replication.
method: A whole-cell modeling framework with cytosolic volume (well-stirred reactor) and membrane surface, where volume and surface area are coupled to reaction dynamics.
finding: Two self-replicating protocell models were constructed; growth and division arise naturally from chemical mechanisms without external enforcement, achieving stable oscillations synchronized to volume and surface area doubling.
dataset: Prototypical whole-cell models (minimal and eukaryotic cell cycle)
metric: Stable oscillations in concentrations and surface-to-volume ratio
result: Self-replicative behavior achieved via synchronized oscillations

problem: Comparing reductive reactivities of microbial and mammalian cytochromes P-450 with heme and whole-cell models for biodehalogenation.
method: Comparative biochemical assays of cytochromes P-450 reductive dehalogenation activity across microbial, mammalian, heme, and whole-cell systems.
dataset: Microbial and mammalian cytochromes P-450, heme models

problem: Current meniscal lesion treatments fail to prevent adverse effects; tissue engineering may offer an alternative for whole meniscus regeneration.
method: Biodegradable PGA scaffolds reinforced with PLGA, seeded with allogeneic meniscal cells, cultured in vitro and transplanted into rabbit knee joints after total meniscectomy.
finding: Neomenisci formed with fibrocartilage regeneration at 6-10 weeks, with type I and II collagen similar to normal tissue, though differences in collagen content and aggregate modulus remained at 36 weeks.
dataset: Rabbit total meniscectomy model
metric: Compressive modulus, collagen content, aggregate modulus, immunohistochemistry
result: 28-fold higher compressive modulus for bonded vs nonbonded scaffold; neomenisci collagen and modulus differ from native

problem: Understanding how inherent randomness in biomolecular movements and reactivity gives rise to large-scale behavioral differences in otherwise identical cells.
method: Lattice Microbes (GPU-accelerated stochastic reaction-diffusion simulator) for microscopic kinetics, and population flux balance analysis (FBA) for systems-level metabolic phenotype variability.
finding: Population FBA predicted variability in E. coli metabolic pathway usage from protein expression heterogeneity; hybrid FBA-kinetic simulations enable study of cell competition and cooperation in colonies.
dataset: Escherichia coli

problem: Little is known about the bioavailability of carotenoids from whole foods despite epidemiological evidence linking carotenoid-rich diets to reduced chronic disease risk.
method: In vitro digestion coupled with Caco-2 human intestinal cell culture model to assess carotenoid absorption from carrots and corn.
finding: Caco-2 uptake of beta-carotene and zeaxanthin was higher than lutein; uptake was curvilinear and saturated after 4 hours; cooked corn significantly enhanced carotenoid bioavailability.
dataset: Carrots, whole grain corn, Caco-2 cells
metric: Cellular uptake kinetics, dose response
result: Saturation at 4h incubation; cooked corn enhanced bioavailability

problem: Interpreting complex multi-compartment metabolomic and genotype data for individual patients remains an unresolved challenge for personalized medicine.
method: Multi-omic, data-driven, personalized whole-cell kinetic models of erythrocyte metabolism constructed from fasting-state plasma/erythrocyte metabolomics and whole-genome genotyping for 24 healthy individuals.
finding: Personalized kinetic rate constants better represent genotype than metabolite levels; models identified individuals at risk for ribavirin-induced anemia and showed how ITPA deficiency may protect against this side effect.
dataset: 24 healthy individuals, plasma/erythrocyte metabolomics, whole-genome genotyping
metric: Genotype-phenotype correlation, drug side effect risk prediction
result: Kinetic rate constants outperform metabolite levels for genotype representation

problem: Reconstructing whole-cell models for systems-level understanding of cellular function.
The t(11;14) translocation alone is insufficient for mantle cell lymphoma (MCL); secondary genomic alterations need to be comprehensively identified.
method: Whole genome array CGH using a DNA microarray of 32,433 overlapping genomic segments spanning the entire human genome, applied to 8 MCL cell lines.
finding: An average of 35 genetic alterations per cell line were identified, with recurrent amplifications at 18q21 (BCL2) and 13q31 (GPC5), homozygous deletion at 9p21 (p15/p16), and 13 novel minimally altered regions.
dataset: 8 MCL cell lines (Granta-519, HBL-2, NCEB-1, Rec-1, SP49, UPN-1, Z138C, JVM-2)
metric: Tiling resolution, recurrent gain/loss detection
result: Average 35 alterations/cell line, 14 recurrent losses, 21 recurrent gains, 13 novel regions, detection down to 130 kb

problem: Building 3D structural models of entire cells requires unprecedented integration of multiple biological data sources and enhanced computational methods.
method: CellPACK suite for computational modeling, combining experimental and homology-modeled structures with lattice-based genome models to build 3D models of a whole Mycoplasma genitalium cell.
finding: First 3D structural models of an entire M. genitalium cell were built; both manual and automated workflows are appropriate for mesoscale analysis of crowding and volume occupancy.
dataset: Mycoplasma genitalium proteome, DNA, RNA
metric: Model quality, volume occupancy, crowding analysis
result: First complete 3D structural model of M. genitalium

problem: Population density approaches to modeling local control of Ca2+-induced Ca2+ release scale poorly with the number of ryanodine receptors per Ca2+ release unit.
method: Langevin description of collective RyR gating coupled by local Ca2+ concentration, combined with Fokker-Planck equations for a local/global whole cell model of permeabilized ventricular myocytes.
finding: Elevated myoplasmic Ca2+ promotes SR Ca2+ uptake but also activates RyRs; increasing myoplasmic Ca2+ causes exponential increase in spark-mediated release and linear increase in nonspark-mediated release, consistent with experiments.
dataset: Permeabilized ventricular myocytes (model)
metric: Spark-mediated vs nonspark-mediated release flux
result: Exponential increase in spark-mediated release, linear increase in nonspark-mediated release; two steady-state release fluxes

problem: Investigating the direct role of TNF-alpha in obesity-linked insulin resistance.
method: TNF-alpha mRNA expression analysis in adipose tissue from four rodent models of obesity/diabetes, plus TNF-alpha neutralization experiments in obese fa/fa rats.
finding: TNF-alpha mRNA and protein were elevated in adipose tissue of obese rodent models; neutralization of TNF-alpha significantly increased peripheral glucose uptake in response to insulin.
dataset: Four rodent models of obesity and diabetes, obese fa/fa rats
metric: Peripheral glucose uptake in response to insulin
baseline: Obese fa/fa rats without TNF-alpha neutralization
result: Significant increase in insulin-stimulated glucose uptake after TNF-alpha neutralization

problem: Tracking the evolving shape of whole fluorescent cells in time-lapse microscopy is challenging due to noise and complex morphologies.
method: Coherence-enhancing diffusion filtering combined with Chan-Vese model minimization in fast level set-like and graph cut frameworks, integrated with a topological prior for multiple cell tracking.
finding: The tracking scheme successfully segments and tracks multiple cells in 2D and 3D time-lapse series of rat adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells and human lung squamous cell carcinoma cells.
dataset: Rat adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells (2D), human lung squamous cell carcinoma cells (3D)

problem: Understanding the uptake, trafficking, and toxic effects of plastic nanoparticles at cellular and systemic levels in aquatic species.
method: Zebrafish (Danio rerio) cell line and larvae exposure to polystyrene nanoparticles of different sizes (50 nm and 1 μm), with analysis of endocytic mechanisms, ROS, gene expression, and immune cell migration.
finding: 50 nm NPs distributed homogeneously in cytoplasm via dynamin-dependent internalization; 1 μm NPs agglomerated via phagocytosis; both degraded in lysosomes causing alkalinization; NPs accumulated in gut, triggered ROS, and decreased survival in immunodeficient/infected larvae.
dataset: Zebrafish cell line (ZFL), zebrafish larvae (wild-type and immunodeficient)
metric: ROS levels, catalase expression, immune cell migration, survival rate
result: Decreased survival in immunodeficient/infected larvae upon NP exposure; lysosomal alkalinization

problem: Integrating computational, algorithmic, and implementational levels of analysis into a single platform for realistic simulation of neural information processing is difficult.
method: Multiscale dynamical 'whole-cell' models for fly photoreceptors integrating molecular-level processes with systems-level function, following Marr's three levels of analysis.
finding: Models uncovered mechanisms and evolutionary rules of quantal light information sampling and integration underlying light adaptation, improving understanding of insect vision.
dataset: Fly (Drosophila) photoreceptors

problem: Current acellular pertussis vaccines do not prevent colonization or transmission of B. pertussis; understanding if whole-cell vaccine benefits are generalizable across manufacturers.
method: Baboon model of pertussis comparing three whole-cell vaccines from different manufacturers against acellular vaccine and unvaccinated controls, measuring nasopharyngeal clearance and IL-17 transcription.
finding: All three whole-cell vaccines significantly accelerated B. pertussis clearance and reduced nasopharyngeal burden; whole-cell vaccines induced IL-17 (Th17) responses unlike acellular vaccines; previously infected animals were not colonized upon reinfection.
dataset: Baboon model, B. pertussis challenge
metric: Nasopharyngeal clearance rate, B. pertussis burden, IL-17 transcription
result: Significant acceleration of clearance and reduction of burden with all 3 whole-cell vaccines

problem: The default constant-volume, steady-state reactor framework is unsatisfactory for modeling growth and division of cells.
method: A whole-cell modeling framework with expanding volumes (cytoplasm and membrane), osmotic pressure-driven growth, and a cell-division cycle with equatorial constriction.
finding: The framework itself imposes periodic/oscillatory behavior on all components due to differential volume change rates, yielding more realistic concentration dynamics than constant-volume models.
dataset: Symbolic whole cell model (spherical cell)
metric: Periodicity, stability of oscillatory solutions
result: Stable periodic solutions without requiring inherent biochemical oscillations

problem: Developing a whole-cell biosensor for screening toxins, using histamine as a model.
method: Potentiometric biosensor with HUVEC monolayer on RGD-modified cellulose triacetate membrane over an ion-selective electrode; toxin-induced permeability changes allow ion diffusion and measurable potential response.
finding: Measured potential increased with histamine concentration while L-histidine did not affect permeability, demonstrating selectivity; applicable to medical, food, environmental, and security fields.
dataset: HUVEC monolayer, histamine and L-histidine
metric: Potentiometric response (mV), selectivity vs L-histidine
result: Dose-dependent response to histamine; no response to L-histidine

problem: Understanding the intracellular distribution and translocation dynamics of the glucocorticoid receptor in GH1 cells.
method: Dense amino acid labeling, sucrose gradient sedimentation, and whole cell studies to characterize 10S/4S/3.5S receptor forms and their equilibrium dynamics.
finding: The 10S receptor is an oligomer in rapidly exchanging equilibrium with its 3.5S subunit; hormone binding shifts equilibrium toward the 4S form which binds DNA and translocates to the nucleus. A new equilibrium model for receptor translocation is proposed.
dataset: GH1 rat pituitary cells, triamcinolone acetonide
metric: Sedimentation coefficient (S), receptor half-life
result: Receptor half-life 9.5h; equilibrium between 10S oligomer and 3.5S/4S subunit

problem: Modeling complex genetic regulatory circuits within a variable-volume whole-cell framework is difficult due to hierarchical cellular organization.
method: Lumped dynamic modular representation of a designed bistable switch (two gene expression modules) using Hill-type kinetics in a variable-volume whole-cell framework mimicking E. coli growth.
finding: The approach allows analysis of stability strength, species connectivity, responsiveness, and regulatory efficiency under stationary and dynamic perturbations, though limitations exist.
dataset: E. coli (in silico model)
metric: Stability strength, responsiveness, regulatory efficiency

problem: Understanding the experimentally observed lateral redistribution of platelets in concentrated red blood cell suspensions during channel flow.
method: Immersed Boundary method with lattice Boltzmann fluid calculation to simulate red-blood-cell-induced lateral platelet motion in channel flow.
finding: Simulations capture RBC-induced lateral platelet motion and near-wall enhanced platelet concentration developing within ~400 ms from initial random/uniform distributions.
dataset: Computational model of whole blood in channel flow
metric: Platelet concentration profile, near-wall concentration enhancement
baseline: Random RBC distribution, uniform platelet distribution (initial condition)
result: Near-wall enhanced platelet concentration develops within ~400 ms

problem: Designing a whole-cell bioluminescence biosensor for water toxicity detection with sensitive optical detection.
method: Genetically engineered E. coli (recA::luxCDABE) with single photon avalanche diode (SPAD) in Geiger mode; mathematical model for SOS bioluminescence kinetics.
finding: Bioluminescence signal initially depends on time squared and spectral intensity is inversely proportional to frequency; excellent agreement between model and measured signal; matched digital filter improved detection.
dataset: E. coli recA::luxCDABE, toxicant concentrations (5, 15, 20 ppm)
metric: Bioluminescence signal intensity, time-dependence
result: Successful detection at 5, 15, and 20 ppm; signal ∝ t²

problem: Developing in vitro 3D culture systems that generate complex organ-like structures (organoids) from stem cells for biomedical research.
method: 3D culture systems enabling stem cell-derived or tissue progenitor cells to self-organize into organoids, building on classical reaggregation experiments.
finding: Organoids create significant prospects for modeling development and diseases, toxicology, drug discovery, and regenerative medicine, though current limitations remain.
dataset: Human stem cells, patient-derived iPSCs

problem: Assessing the cytotoxicity and genotoxicity of Moringa oleifera seeds used as a coagulation reagent for drinking water purification.
method: Acinetobacter whole-cell bioreporter for cytotoxicity and genotoxicity assessment; direct contact, ethanolic-water extraction, and hexane extraction to distinguish hydrophobic/hydrophilic toxic components.
finding: Hydrophobic lipids contributed dominant cytotoxicity; genotoxicity was dominant in the water-soluble fraction; toxicity showed remarkable synergistic effects.
dataset: Moringa oleifera seeds (1-1000 mg/L), Acinetobacter bioreporter
metric: LC50, LC90, genotoxicity equivalence
result: LC50=8.5 mg/L, LC90=300 mg/L, genotoxicity equivalent to 8.3 mg mitomycin C per 1.0 g dry seed

problem: Establishing an in vitro model for studying programmed cell death during mammary gland involution.
method: Comparison of DNA fragmentation and gene expression in involuting mouse mammary glands in vivo vs whole organ culture in vitro with lactogenic hormone withdrawal.
finding: Non-random DNA degradation and similar gene expression patterns (beta-casein, TGF-beta 1/2/3) were observed both in vivo and in vitro, validating whole organ culture as a useful model for studying involution.
dataset: Mouse mammary glands (in vivo and whole organ culture)
metric: DNA fragmentation patterns, gene expression (beta-casein, TGF-beta 1/2/3)
result: Similar DNA degradation and gene expression patterns in vivo and in vitro

problem: Determining the impact of switching from whole-cell to acellular pertussis vaccines on the incidence of B. parapertussis infections.
method: Mouse respiratory model comparing two commercial acellular and two whole-cell vaccines for efficacy against B. parapertussis.
finding: Whole-cell vaccines protected significantly better than acellular vaccines against B. parapertussis; acellular vaccines showed highly significant enhancement of B. parapertussis colonization later in infection.
dataset: Mouse respiratory model, B. parapertussis, B. pertussis
metric: Colonization levels, protection efficacy
result: Whole-cell vaccines significantly better than acellular; acellular vaccines enhanced B. parapertussis colonization

problem: Assessing the digestive stability and intestinal absorption of lycopene and alpha-tocopherol from a whole food.
method: Dynamic TNO gastrointestinal tract model (TIM) coupled with Caco-2 cell monolayers for the first time to evaluate bioaccessibility and absorption from red tomato and sunflower oil.
finding: Alpha-tocopherol was stable during digestion but lycopene showed 25% loss; absorption of both was curvilinear, bidirectional, and concentration-dependent; lycopene showed lower bioavailability than alpha-tocopherol.
dataset: Red tomatoes and sunflower oil, Caco-2 cells, TIM system
metric: Digestive stability, absorption percentage, dose-response
baseline: Pure compounds vs whole food digesta
result: 25% lycopene loss during digestion; lower bioavailability for lycopene vs alpha-tocopherol

problem: Understanding the roles of heat-shock proteins (Hsps) and molecular chaperones in stress responses across ecological and evolutionary contexts.
method: Review of Hsp expression studies in model and nonmodel organisms under natural and experimental stress conditions.
finding: Hsp expression occurs in nature, varies across species, correlates with stress resistance, and species' thresholds for Hsp expression correlate with natural stress levels.
dataset: Multiple model and nonmodel eukaryotic organisms
result: Hsp expression thresholds correlate with natural stress levels

problem: Developing a whole-cell model of ribosome biogenesis focusing on kinetic modeling of small subunit (SSU) assembly.
Bridging the gap between 2D cell cultures and whole-animal systems for studying tissue morphogenesis, stem cells, and cancer.
method: Three-dimensional in vitro models mimicking features of the in vivo environment, combined with traditional cell culture tools.
finding: 3D models provide unique perspectives on stem cell behavior, developing tissues/organs, and tumors, and may accelerate translational research in cancer biology and tissue engineering.

problem: Developing a contactless dielectrophoresis (cDEP) platform capable of continuously sorting cancer cells from dilute whole blood samples.
method: cDEP device with fluid electrodes isolated by thin membrane; analytical model as network of parallel RC pairs; finite element methods for geometry evaluation; operation below 100 kHz.
finding: First cDEP device operating below 100 kHz successfully manipulated human leukemia cells while red blood cells were unaffected, enabling continuous sorting from dilute blood.
dataset: Human leukemia cells, dilute whole blood
metric: Cell manipulation at specified frequencies
result: First successful cDEP below 100 kHz; leukemia cells sorted, RBCs unaffected

problem: Quantitatively predicting in vivo hepatic transport and elimination from in vitro uptake data for BDDCS class 3 drugs.
method: Mechanistic modeling of in vitro hepatic uptake transport in plated rat hepatocytes, scaled to a fully mechanistic PBPK model for in vivo elimination prediction.
finding: Scaled in vitro uptake clearance required a 10-fold empirical scaling factor; plasma clearance, half-life, max liver concentration, and bile excretion fraction were predicted within 2-fold.
dataset: Fexofenadine and napsagatran in rat hepatocytes and in vivo rat
metric: Plasma clearance, half-life, Cmax liver, fraction excreted in bile
result: CL_P, t_1/2, C_maxL, f_bile predicted within 2-fold; 10x empirical scaling factor needed

problem: Whole-cell bioluminescent biosensors suffer from inherent problems in overall system light collection efficiency.
method: Optical ray tracing simulation of bioluminescence emission, transfer, and detection in microbial whole cell biochips, optimizing system geometry and bacterial concentration.
finding: Optimal detector radius between 300-750 μm and bacterial fixation radius between 800-1200 μm were identified for maximizing light collection.
dataset: Microbial whole cell biochip (simulated)
metric: Light collection efficiency
result: Optimal detector radius 300-750 μm, bacterial fixation radius 800-1200 μm

problem: How single mitochondrial flickering events and whole-cell mitochondrial oscillations are mechanistically linked remains unclear.
method: Markov model of inner membrane anion channel with ROS-induced opening, coupled into a mitochondrial network model with ROS-induced ROS release.
finding: As superoxide production increases, random flickering transitions through short-lived waves to recurring propagating waves via self-organized criticality (power-law cluster distribution), ultimately producing whole-cell oscillations.
dataset: Mitochondrial network model (in silico)
metric: Power-law distribution of depolarization clusters, periodicity of oscillations
result: Power-law distribution at critical state; transition from random flickering to periodic oscillations

problem: Examining the ability of S. aureus peptidoglycan and lipoteichoic acid to induce cytokine release and identifying the cellular origins of these cytokines.
method: Human whole blood model stimulated with PepG and LTA; immunomagnetic separation of cell populations; RT-PCR for cytokine mRNA; CD14 receptor blockade.
finding: Both T cells and monocytes produce IL-6 and IL-10 in response to PepG; PepG is a potent TNF-alpha inducer; CD14 blockade attenuated LTA-induced but not PepG-induced TNF-alpha release.
dataset: Human whole blood, S. aureus PepG and LTA
metric: Cytokine levels (TNF-alpha, IL-6, IL-10), mRNA transcripts
result: TNF-alpha peak at 6h, IL-10 peak at 12h; dose-dependent response

problem: Isolating and characterizing murine hematopoietic stem cells (HSC) that are replicating in vivo.
method: Hoechst 33342 vital dye staining with dual-emission-wavelength display to identify side population (SP) cells; competitive repopulation experiments; verapamil blockade of MDR-mediated efflux.
finding: SP cells contain the vast majority of HSC activity, are enriched ≥1,000-fold for reconstitution activity, contribute to lymphoid and myeloid lineages, and 1-3% are in S-G2M phase with reconstitution capacity equivalent to quiescent stem cells.
dataset: Murine bone marrow cells
metric: Fold enrichment for reconstitution activity, DNA content (cell cycle)
result: ≥1,000-fold enrichment; 1-3% HSC in S-G2M

problem: Developing a mathematical model of a whole-cell-based bio-chip as an electrochemical biosensor for water toxicity detection.
Deterministic Hodgkin-Huxley models fail to reproduce the irregular intrinsic behavior observed in bursting neurons of the crustacean stomatogastric ganglion.
method: Stochastic Hodgkin-Huxley-type whole cell model based on microscopic Markovian ion channel states, analyzed with nonlinear dynamics tools.
finding: The stochastic model reproduces the irregularities, sensitivity to initial conditions, and low-dimensional dynamics found in isolated STG neurons, arising naturally from ion channel stochasticity and nonlinear interactions.
dataset: Crustacean stomatogastric ganglion (STG) neuron model
metric: Sensitivity to initial conditions, dimensionality of dynamics
result: Stochastic model reproduces experimental irregularities without unrealistic dynamics

problem: Modeling the entire expanded-bed adsorption process for protein purification directly from yeast cell-containing feedstock.
method: Mathematical model with independently determined parameters for expanded bed adsorption of BSA from yeast cell suspensions using DEAE Spherodex M anion-exchanger; steric mass-action isotherm for elution.
finding: Dynamic binding capacity of BSA reached 86% of equilibrium despite 6% yeast cells due to small adsorbent size and high pore diffusivity; model predictions agreed well with experimental results for the whole process.
dataset: BSA with 6% yeast cell suspension, DEAE Spherodex M
metric: Dynamic binding capacity, axial dispersion coefficient
result: 86% dynamic binding capacity; axial dispersion ~10⁻⁶ m²/s; good model-experiment agreement

problem: Determining the influence of aging and heat treatment on the ability of whole yeast cells and yeast cell walls to remove ochratoxin A from wine.
method: Wine model system with 10 μg/L OTA, 1 g/L whole yeast cells or yeast cell walls, monitored over 214 hours with and without heat pretreatment (85°C, 10 min).
finding: YCW mannoproteins were involved in OTA removal via adsorption; aging affected WY but not YCW removal capacity; heat treatment increased removal capacity for both.
dataset: Wine model system, OTA (10 μg/L), whole yeast cells and yeast cell walls
metric: OTA removal capacity
result: Heat treatment (85°C, 10 min) increased OTA removal capacity

problem: Investigating allogeneic whole-tumour cell vaccination as cancer immunotherapy in a rat prostate cancer model.
method: Crossover vaccination of irradiated MAT-LyLu and PAIII tumour cells between Copenhagen and Lobund-Wistar rats, with SRL172 (heat-killed M. vaccae) as adjuvant.
finding: Prophylactic allogeneic vaccination provided 80% survival >5 months (vs 40% autologous) with prolonged immunity; SRL172 adjuvant was needed against the more aggressive tumour; therapeutic vaccination showed limited regression.
dataset: Rat prostate cancer (MAT-LyLu, PAIII cell lines), Copenhagen and Lobund-Wistar rats
